links.title: Protect Stockton's Street Dogs
links.descriptionLong:
The Stockton street dog situation looks like something out of a third world country, not the economic powerhouse of the United States.
Hundreds of dogs are chained, starving, and dehydrated. No food, no water, no shelter, no room to move. Abuse is happening openly while the people in charge stand by and watch.
In just a couple of weekends, two separate brand-new litters of puppies appeared and had to be rushed into homes so they didn’t end up dead or back in the same nightmare.
And through all of it, there are only two consistent presences on the ground: Jesse, of @StocktonStreetDogs and @cydnikeala, the only people trying to hold back a tidal wave of suffering while those with the power and the budgets look away and line their pockets with taxpayer money.
links.mailtoSubject: Stockton’s street dogs
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing with deep concern to demand immediate and sustained action from Stockton city leadership and San Joaquin County officials to protect the dogs on Stockton’s streets, in homeless encampments, and inside Stockton Animal Shelter.

Right now, what is happening is simple and indefensible:

* Stockton has a growing population of free-roaming and encampment dogs.
* Healthy, young, adoptable puppies with no serious medical or behavioral issues are being collected by Animal Services and euthanised within roughly 48 hours of intake.
* Dogs removed from clearly dangerous situations have been adopted or returned back into the hands of the same people who mistreated them.

That is a system that is choosing the fastest and cheapest options at the expense of lives that could be saved.

Between the City of Stockton, San Joaquin County, Stockton Police Department, the Sheriff’s Office, and the District Attorney’s Office, taxpayers are funding millions of dollars in salaries and operating budgets that are supposed to deliver public safety and humane animal care. Instead, residents see:

* Free-roaming and encampment dogs breeding unchecked, without consistent spay/neuter, vaccination, or outreach
* Healthy, adoptable dogs and puppies killed within days of arrival at the shelter
* Abused and neglected dogs recycled back to the same people who harmed them
* Little visible enforcement against clear cruelty and neglect
* No regular, transparent reporting on how many animals are taken in, how many are killed, and why

If this continues, Stockton is at serious risk of becoming a domestic version of the worst international examples, where “stray management” means mass round-ups and quiet culls. Turkey, Morocco, and India are already criticised worldwide for answering public pressure on street dogs with poisonings and killings instead of real reform. Stockton does not need to follow that model.

These dogs are not disposable. They feel pain, fear, and attachment, and they deserve a chance at life just as much as the next person does. With the authority and budgets you control, there is no honest excuse for leaving them to be abused on the street, recycled to abusers, or killed in the shelter for convenience or space.

I urge you to take the following actions without delay:

1. End rapid killing of healthy, adoptable animals.
Immediately halt the euthanasia of healthy, treatable, and behaviorally sound dogs and puppies for “space” or convenience. Review and revise hold-time and euthanasia policies at Stockton Animal Shelter so that healthy, adoptable animals are not being killed within 48 hours of intake, and ensure full compliance with California law and the intent of the Hayden Act.

2. Stop returning animals to abusers.
Implement and enforce a policy that prevents animals from being adopted or returned to people with documented histories of abuse, neglect, or repeated welfare complaints. This must include basic cross-checking of adoption and surrender records with cruelty reports, police records, and court outcomes, and a clear process for permanently flagging high-risk individuals.

3. Treat cruelty and neglect as enforceable crime.
Direct Stockton PD, Animal Services, the Sheriff’s Office, and the District Attorney’s Office to treat serious cruelty and neglect as criminal violations that carry real consequences, not as background noise. Track and publicly report the number of animal cruelty complaints, investigations, citations, and prosecutions each year so that residents can see whether laws are actually being enforced.

4. Create a humane plan for street and encampment dogs.
Develop a written strategy for dogs living on the streets and in homeless encampments that is based on prevention and support, not round-ups and killing. This must include on-site spay/neuter, vaccination, microchipping, voluntary surrender options, and support for people who are genuinely trying to care for their animals. Any attempt to “clean up” by sweeping dogs off the streets to be killed will be recognised for what it is: a cull.

5. Attach real conditions to county funding and contracts.
As long as San Joaquin County continues to use and help fund Stockton Animal Shelter, the Board of Supervisors, County Counsel, and the Sheriff’s Office share responsibility for what happens there. The county’s contract with Stockton should require humane, law-compliant policies on hold times, euthanasia, rescue transfers, and transparency as a condition of ongoing funding, and those conditions should be enforced.

6. Publish regular, accessible data.
Commit to publishing clear monthly or quarterly reports showing intake, adoption, transfer, reclaim, euthanasia (with reasons), and average length of stay for animals at Stockton Animal Shelter. Without basic data, there can be no real accountability.

You cannot credibly claim to care about vulnerable communities or responsible use of public funds while allowing dogs to be abused in public, sent back to abusers, and killed inside a publicly funded shelter in a matter of days.

The choice in front of you is clear: reform this system into something humane, lawful, and transparent, or accept full responsibility when Stockton becomes the example people point to for how not to handle street dogs.

Please act now.

Sincerely,

targets: Stockton & San Joaquin Authorities & Decision Makers

phones.who: San Joaquin County Board of Supervisors Office

links.title: Protect Abused and Neglected Dogs on Skid Row
links.descriptionLong:
While LA battles homelessness, forgotten victims walk on four legs. Starving, abused, sold, neglected, and invisible. Dogs tied to shopping carts. Puppies dying in the heat. No food, no vet care, no mercy.

Demand action NOW from LA city/county officials:
Investigate abuse.
Offer outreach vet care.
Hold abusers accountable.
Don’t force people to choose between housing and their pets.
links.mailtoSubject: Skid Row animal cruelty: $95M in budgets, $2.5M in salaries, and no protection
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing with deep concern to demand immediate and sustained action from the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors to protect the abused and neglected animals living in and around Skid Row.

For years, the County and City have acknowledged the humanitarian crisis on Skid Row. Yet the animals trapped in the same streets and camps remain almost entirely unprotected. That is not an accident; it is the result of choices made at the top.

Between you and other key officials responsible for public safety and animal welfare in Los Angeles, taxpayers are funding around $2.5 million a year in salaries alone. On top of that, the combined city and county animal control/animal services budgets are roughly $95 million annually. For that level of investment, the public is entitled to expect at least basic enforcement of existing animal cruelty laws and a visible effort to prevent suffering.

Instead, on Skid Row, countless dogs are:

* Chronically malnourished and dehydrated
* Left without basic medical care, vaccinations, or sterilization
* Subjected to violent abuse, crude restraints, and intimidation
* Exposed to forced drug use and the risk of overdose
* Kept for years in extreme heat and cold without adequate shelter
* Living in dangerous, unsanitary conditions with no meaningful oversight

These are not unfortunate "edge cases." They are ongoing violations of California Penal Code 597. The fact that this is happening in a poverty-stricken area does not make it legal and does not make it acceptable.

When nearly $95 million is being poured into animal services and enforcement, and the result on the ground is starving, injured, drugged, and abused animals in full public view on Skid Row, that looks very much like a gross misuse and mismanagement of public funds under your watch. When officials paid high six-figure salaries cannot ensure even basic enforcement of the law in one of the most visible crisis zones in the county, it is fair to say they are overpaid for the job they are actually doing.

You cannot credibly claim to care about vulnerable communities while allowing their animals to be starved, beaten, traded, and disposed of in plain sight.

I urge you to take the following actions without further delay:

1. Create a dedicated city-county task force focused specifically on animal abuse and neglect in and around Skid Row, with the authority to investigate, seize animals in danger, and refer cases for prosecution.
2. Fund and expand veterinary outreach on Skid Row, including on-site medical care, vaccinations, spay/neuter, and access to food and basic supplies for guardians who are genuinely trying to care for their animals.
3. Enforce California Penal Code 597 consistently, regardless of ZIP code or housing status. Abuse and deliberate cruelty on Skid Row must result in real consequences, not warnings and case numbers that go nowhere.
4. Implement and support co-sheltering policies countywide so that people do not have to choose between accepting housing and keeping their animals safe. No one should be forced to leave a pet in dangerous conditions because the system has no place for them together.
5. Publish regular, transparent reports on animal cruelty complaints, response times, and case outcomes in the Skid Row area, so the public can see whether this crisis is finally being treated as a priority rather than an embarrassment to be ignored.

Animal cruelty is never excusable. It is not made acceptable by poverty, addiction, or bureaucratic complexity. With the salaries and budgets you control, there is no honest excuse for leaving animals on Skid Row to starve, suffer, and die without meaningful intervention.

Please act now, and demonstrate that Los Angeles County will no longer tolerate this level of suffering for any living being in its care.

Sincerely,

phones.who: LA County Supervisor Hilda L. Solis
LA County Supervisor Holly J. Mitchell
LA County Supervisor Lindsey P. Horvath
LA City Attorney Hydee Feldstein Soto
Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman

links.title: Stop the Mass Slaughter of Street Dogs in India
links.descriptionLong:
On August 11th, 2025, India’s Supreme Court ordered all stray dogs in Delhi-NCR to be removed from public spaces and locked away in shelters, with no return to their home streets.

Even though a later bench called that “too harsh” and talked about sterilise-vaccinate-return with designated feeding zones, the damage was done. People heard exactly what they wanted to hear: the dogs are the problem, and getting rid of them is acceptable.

Since then, we’ve seen what that looks like on the ground.
Dogs beaten with sticks and iron rods.
Dogs rounded up and dumped in places with no food, water or shelter.
A dog in Jaipur tied by the legs and thrown onto a live transformer, electrocuted to death while his caretaker screamed.

This is state-sanctioned cruelty by silence.

India’s Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act still values a dog’s suffering at ₹10-50 ($0.11-0.56) for a first offence. Even repeat offenders can walk away with a token fine and a few months in jail, if that.

TNVR, trap, neuter, vaccinate, return, works. It reduces bites. It controls rabies. It stabilises street dog populations. But killing, dumping and looking the other way is cheaper in the short term. Once again, money matters more than life.

India loves to sell itself as the land of ahimsa, of compassion for all living beings. Right now, its streets tell a very different story.

India’s tourism industry is projected to pump tens of billions into the economy in 2025. If the government and the courts want to ignore the blood on the pavements, then it’s time to hit them where it hurts most, their wallet.

Cancel the yoga retreats and tiger safaris.
Skip the spiritual “bucket list” tours.
Choose countries that actually enforce cruelty laws.

Until India stops turning its street dogs into punching bags and corpses, it does not deserve your money.
links.mailtoSubject: Delhi stray dog rulings are fuelling nationwide cruelty
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing to express serious concern about how recent decisions and messaging on street dogs in Delhi and across India are playing out on the ground.

In August 2025, the Supreme Court's order directing Delhi-NCR civic bodies to remove all stray dogs from public spaces and confine them to shelters, with no return to their home territories, sent a very clear message to the public: street dogs are a "menace" to be cleared away, not sentient beings whose welfare is protected by law.

Even on the narrow question of designated feeding points, the courts and authorities have dragged their heels. Feeders, RWAs and municipal staff have been left without clear, enforceable instructions, and that vacuum is being filled by conflict, harassment of feeders and open violence towards the dogs themselves.

Even though a later bench recognised that this approach was "too harsh" and reaffirmed the Animal Birth Control (ABC) Rules, sterilise, vaccinate, deworm and return dogs to the same locations, with designated feeding points, the damage is already done. The initial order has been read on the ground as a green light for cruelty, not humane management.

Since that ruling, activists and local witnesses have documented a pattern of vigilante behaviour that should horrify any government that claims to uphold the rule of law:

- Street dogs being beaten with sticks and iron rods.
- Dogs being rounded up, transported long distances and dumped in areas where they cannot find food, water, shelter or their feeders.
- At least one case of a dog's legs being tied together and the animal thrown onto a transformer.
- Communities openly boasting about "getting rid of" dogs rather than demanding sterilisation and vaccination drives.

These are not "dog control measures". They are acts of deliberate torture.

At the same time, your own laws continue to tell abusers that animal cruelty is basically free. Under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960, the fine for a first offence can be as low as ₹10-50, literally less than a bus fare, and even repeat offenders can walk away with as little as ₹20-100 or a maximum of three months' imprisonment. The Government of India has itself acknowledged in Parliament that these penalties are still in force and woefully outdated.

You cannot seriously expect people to respect animals when the law values a dog's suffering at less than a cup of tea.

This pattern is not unique. We have already watched what happens when governments send a signal that street animals are disposable:

- In Turkey, policy changes and court decisions dismantling long-standing neuter/return strategies were followed by sporadic street dog massacres and mob violence against animals.
- In Morocco, authorities have been shooting, poisoning and rounding up dogs in the streets, often in front of children, in an attempt to "clean up" cities, prompting global condemnation and warnings about the psychological harm to children who witness this brutality.

India is now at the same crossroads. You can either double down on a narrative that turns citizens into vigilantes and dogs into targets, or you can treat this as a chance to lead with humane, science-based policy.

I am therefore calling on you to take the following steps, urgently and publicly:

ISSUE A CLEAR NATIONAL STATEMENT AGAINST VIGILANTE CRUELTY

- Clarify that no private citizen, RWA, security guard or municipal worker has any authority to beat, kill, abandon or illegally relocate community dogs.
- Reaffirm that the only lawful framework for managing street dog populations is sterilisation, vaccination and return in line with the ABC Rules, with humane handling and proper shelter standards for genuinely dangerous or rabid animals.

DIRECT POLICE AND CIVIC BODIES TO TREAT CRUELTY AS CRIME, NOT "NUISANCE"

- Mandate that all complaints of beating, poisoning, dumping or torture of dogs be registered as FIRs under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act and relevant IPC/BNS provisions (including for maiming and killing animals), rather than being dismissed as "local disputes".
- Require time-bound, publicly reportable action: investigation, seizure of evidence (including CCTV and mobile footage), veterinary reports, and prosecution.

IMMEDIATELY STRENGTHEN PENALTIES FOR ANIMAL CRUELTY

- Fast-track the long-delayed amendments to the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act to bring penalties into the 21st century, meaningful fines, custodial sentences and making serious cruelty cognisable, so police can act without waiting for bureaucracy to catch up.
- Ensure that offences involving organised dumping, mass killing or extreme torture carry penalties that actually deter, not symbolic fines of a few rupees.

IMPLEMENT ABC AND RABIES-CONTROL PROGRAMMES NATIONWIDE, WITH TRANSPARENCY

- Publish data, by city and state, on:
  - Number of dogs sterilised and vaccinated.
  - Dog bite incidents and post-exposure prophylaxis coverage.
  - Number of cruelty FIRs registered and their outcomes.
- Allow independent audits of shelters and pounds to ensure they are not simply kill-centres under another name.

ENGAGE WITH FRONTLINE ANIMAL WELFARE EXPERTS INSTEAD OF SIDELINING THEM

- Involve credible Indian animal welfare organisations, veterinary experts and public-health professionals in designing any revised stray-dog policy so that it protects both human safety and animal welfare.
- Stop treating feeders and rescuers as the problem. They are often the only people on the ground vaccinating, sterilising and socialising these dogs at their own expense.

Right now, the message many people are hearing is: "The Supreme Court and the government are on our side if we want the dogs gone, and nobody will punish us if we are cruel." That message is already costing dogs their lives. It is also eroding respect for law, fuelling social conflict, and exposing children to terrifying violence that will stay with them for life.

India presents itself to the world as a nation built on a rich civilisation, deep cultural traditions and a constitutional commitment to justice. Hindu philosophy and wider Indian thought have long emphasised values such as ahimsa and compassion toward all living beings. Those are not abstract slogans; they are supposed to shape how the most vulnerable are treated, including animals who depend entirely on human mercy.

When street dogs are beaten, dumped or tortured with impunity, it does not only betray those animals. It betrays India's own history and the values it claims to stand for. A country that takes pride in its culture, its spirituality and its respect for the rule of law cannot look the other way while cruelty is normalised on its streets and those responsible face almost no consequences.

If India wants to be taken seriously as a nation that respects both the rule of law and basic compassion, this pattern of tolerated cruelty cannot continue.

I urge you to act now, not with more vague assurances, but with concrete, public steps: stronger laws, real enforcement against abusers, and a nationwide reaffirmation that cruelty to animals is neither legal, nor acceptable, nor consequence-free.

The world is watching what you do next.

Sincerely,

links.title: Stop The Cat and Dog Meat Trade in China
links.descriptionLong:
China removed dogs from the national livestock list in 2020. Because there are no national animal welfare laws in China, dogs now have no legal rights.

No food animal status means no food-safety framework, no lawful slaughter rules, and no transport standards, resulting in a black market of stolen pets, back-alley killing, menu fraud, and trucks moving animals across the country in the dark.

A superstitious belief that more pain means better meat, and no binding standards, leads to dogs boiled alive, blowtorched alive, skinned alive, and savagely beaten.

Shenzhen and Zhuhai banned it outright. The rest of the country hasn’t.
So dogs and cats now exist in a loophole: not approved as food, not banned everywhere, which means fewer protections and more suffering.

"It's their culture/tradition" is a copout and ignorance talking. You wouldn't give a free pass to "cultural/traditional" child brides, slavery, or honor killings elsewhere.
China needs a national ban on slaughter, transport, sale, and advertising. Licenses pulled. Markets closed. Raids on supply lines. Public enforcement reports.

links.mailtoSubject: Arrest Animal Abuser Najae Marie Vanderhurst
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing as a concerned citizen to formally request a prompt investigation into allegations of animal cruelty involving Najae Marie Vanderhurst of Douglasville, Georgia.

According to public reports and a captured video from her Instagram story (posted around 3 PM on or about the date referenced in recent posts), Ms. Vanderhurst appears to be pouring water on a dog's head while the animal is confined in a very small cage. She reportedly captioned or commented on the video with statements indicating frustration and intent, such as "I hate this dog... all she do is bark and that's why I wet her stupid ass up!" A child is also visible laughing in the video. The video has since been deleted, but screenshots and recordings have been shared online.

Under Georgia law (O.C.G.A. Section 16-12-4), animal cruelty is a serious offense, and acts involving unnecessary physical harm, confinement in inadequate conditions, or abusive treatment warrant investigation. The presence of a minor in the video also raises additional concerns regarding the environment.

I urge your agency to:
- Immediately investigate these allegations.
- Locate and check the welfare of the dog involved.
- Review any available evidence, including the captured video and related social media posts.
- Take appropriate enforcement action if violations are confirmed, including potential criminal charges.

Thank you for your attention to this important animal welfare issue.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1rWXiVBndfyZ8SjUT1xX5KYtzQXzOzinT/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Recue Tethered Dog at 3112 S Western Ave, LA
links.descriptionLong:
Location: 3112 S Western Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90018

This dog was left out all day in 90°F heat and well into the night.
Leaving a dog tethered outside with no adequate shade, water, or relief breaches California Health & Safety Code § 122335, California Penal Code § 597, § 597t, and Los Angeles Municipal Code § 53.70.
Penal Code 597.1 allows police officers and animal control officers to seize animals based on suspected abuse/neglect, not just confirmed.
links.mailtoSubject: Dog Left Tied Outside in Extreme Heat at 3112 S Western Ave
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing to report clear animal neglect and abuse at 3112 S Western Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90018. A dog has been tied up outside this property all day in temperatures reaching 90F and remains there into the night with no adequate shade, water, or relief from the heat.

This is not an isolated case. It is part of a growing pattern in Los Angeles where animal cruelty and neglect go unaddressed, making the city's animal welfare enforcement a joke.

Leaving a dog tethered in extreme heat without proper care violates Los Angeles Municipal Code and California law. California Penal Code allows peace officers and animal control officers to seize and impound an animal based on suspected abuse or neglect when there is reasonable belief that prompt action is needed to protect the animal's health or safety. Confirmation of abuse is not required for immediate intervention.

I and many concerned residents demand that LA Animal Services take immediate action:

- Dispatch officers to 3112 S Western Ave today to check on the dog and seize it from the harmful situation if needed.
- Investigate the owners and hold them fully accountable under the law.
- Resolve this case promptly.

Citizen of Los Angeles and beyond are tired of repeated failures by LA Animal Services to protect animals in distress. This situation must be sorted out now with no more excuses.

Thank you for your urgent attention to this matter.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/11DspwgrsXU5lCAhj_3UcAyWxi3eTwroC/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAniamls Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Release Wayne Hsiung & Associates
links.descriptionLong:
Activists today rescued 22 beagles from Ridglan Farms, a facility that breeds beagles for the "purpose" of animal testing.
Multiple activists were arrested, including co-founder of @directactioneverywhere DxE @waynehhsiung & former Baywatch actress @thealexandrapaul.
Our kids are raised to be compassionate and kind to others, but adults are being arrested for the exact same thing.

Sheriff Kalvin Barrett and his band of thugs at @danectysheriffsoffice seized eight of the beagles to be delivered back to the very abusers they were liberated from.

We are calling for the release of the beagles confiscated and the release of the activists involved without charge. Laws exist to protect the good and punish the wicked, not the other way around.
links.mailtoSubject: Release Wayne Hsiung & Associates
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing as a concerned citizen to demand the immediate release of Wayne Hsiung and all associated activists arrested today, March 15, 2026, during a peaceful open rescue at Ridglan Farms (10489 W Blue Mounds Rd, Blue Mounds, WI). These individuals acted to document and alleviate documented suffering of beagles bred for laboratory experimentation. Continuing to hold them while ignoring the facility’s practices is a miscarriage of justice.  

Ridglan Farms is a private breeder, but its operations are indirectly subsidized by **taxpayer dollars**. Federal agencies like the National Institutes of Health (NIH) provide grants to universities and labs that purchase Ridglan beagles for testing. Public funds are therefore flowing into a system that breeds and uses these dogs in experiments.  

Compounding this misuse of public money is the shocking reality that 90–95% of drugs and treatments that succeed in animal trials fail in human clinical trials. The vast majority of these experiments lead to unnecessary suffering and death for the animals, with no meaningful benefit to human health. We are sacrificing sentient beings on the altar of a system that is scientifically inefficient and ethically indefensible.  

If a private individual conducted the exact same experiments injecting compounds into dogs to test effects in their basement without licenses or oversight, they would face immediate felony animal cruelty charges under Wisconsin law (Wis. Stat. § 951) and the federal Animal Welfare Act. Yet licensed facilities like Ridglan receive legal protections and exemptions simply because they hold the proper paperwork. This double standard allows institutionalized cruelty while punishing those who try to stop it. Animals in these facilities endure conditions that would result in prosecution if inflicted on pet dogs.  

I respectfully demand that you:  
1. Immediately drop all charges and release Wayne Hsiung and the other activists.  
2. Launch a full investigation into Ridglan Farms for violations of animal cruelty statutes, using the evidence provided by today’s rescue.  
3. Order the Dane County Sheriff's Office to return the 8 seized beagles to the care of experienced rescuers or a safe sanctuary, ensuring they are not returned to Ridglan Farms.
4. Prioritize the welfare of the remaining beagles and enforce the law consistently, without favoritism toward licensed experimentation.  

Public trust in our justice system requires equal application of cruelty laws, regardless of industry protections or taxpayer involvement. The moral and fiscal case against continuing these practices is clear. Please act swiftly and provide a public update on steps taken.  

Thank you for your attention to this urgent matter.

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

إلى من يهمه الأمر،

أكتب إليكم لأبلغكم رسمياً عن حادثة مروعة تتعلق بالقسوة على الحيوانات وقعت في شارع سعيد، ميدان الجيش (منطقة الداهر) بوسط القاهرة. تم تصوير رجل في مقطع فيديو وهو يركل كلبًا أعزل عدة مرات في وضح النهار، تاركًا إياه مرعوبًا وغير قادر حتى على النظر إلى من أساء إليه. كان الكلب مربوطًا بسلسلة وياقة، مما يشير إلى أنه قد يكون ملكًا لهذا الشخص. وقف المارة مكتوفي الأيدي دون تدخل، وعانى الكلب، الذي كان في حالة ضعف بالفعل، من الألم والخوف.

لا يمثل هذا الفعل انتهاكًا صارخًا لقوانين مصر فحسب، بل يهدد أيضًا الصحة والسلامة العامة بشكل مباشر.

تواجه مصر أزمة خطيرة تتعلق بالكلاب، حيث أبلغت وزارة الصحة عن أكثر من 1.2 مليون حالة عض أو خدش من قبل الحيوانات في الفترة من يناير إلى سبتمبر 2025 وحدها (العديد منها مرتبط بالكلاب)، وتصل الأرقام إلى ما يصل إلى 1.4 مليون حالة عض من قبل الكلاب على مدار العام بأكمله في بعض التقارير. لا يزال داء الكلب يمثل خطرًا مميتًا، حيث يودي بحياة ما يقرب من 60 شخصًا سنويًا على الرغم من توفر العلاجات بعد التعرض. تساهم أعداد الكلاب (بما في ذلك الكلاب الضالة والحيوانات الأليفة المملوكة) في ارتفاع هذه الأرقام، لكن الإساءة غير المقيدة تفاقم المشكلة.

تخلق هذه القسوة حلقة خطيرة:
- إساءة معاملة الكلب تغرس الخوف العميق والصدمة.
- الكلب المرعوب والمصدوم أكثر عرضة بكثير للرد بشكل دفاعي - بالعض أو العض عند الاقتراب منه أو محاصرته أو شعوره بالتهديد.
- يؤدي هذا إلى المزيد من حالات العض للناس (خاصة الأطفال أو المارة غير المدركين للخطر).
- ثم يُلقى اللوم على الكلب، وغالبًا ما يتم إعدامه أو تسميمه، بينما يظل السبب الجذري - الإساءة - دون عقاب.
- وتستمر الحلقة: المزيد من الخوف من الكلاب، والمزيد من القسوة، والمزيد من مخاطر العدوانية، وزيادة التعرض لداء الكلب.

وبفشلنا في معالجة هذا الإساءة العامة، تظل مجتمعاتنا معرضة للخطر. إن محاسبة الجاني من شأنه أن يردع الإساءة في المستقبل، ويدعم جهود مصر الوطنية لمكافحة داء الكلب (بما في ذلك تطعيم وتخصيص آلاف الكلاب الضالة لتحقيق هدف ”مصر خالية من داء الكلب بحلول عام 2030“)، ويقلل من حوادث العض للجميع.

بموجب قانون العقوبات المصري (مثل المواد 355-357)، يُجرّم الإيذاء المتعمد أو القتل أو القسوة تجاه الحيوانات الأليفة/المستأنسة، مع عقوبات تشمل السجن والغرامات. وقد أكدت النيابة العامة أن إساءة معاملة الحيوانات تنتهك القانون والقيم الدينية والأخلاق الإنسانية، وحثت على إجراء تحقيقات واتخاذ إجراءات صارمة.

أحثكم على:
- تحديد هوية الشخص المسؤول واعتقاله.
- إجراء تحقيق شامل باستخدام الأدلة المقدمة.
- المقاضاة بأقصى ما يسمح به القانون لتكون عبرة للجمهور.
- التنسيق مع الخدمات البيطرية لإنقاذ/إعادة تأهيل الكلب إن أمكن.

يتطلب هذا الحادث اتخاذ إجراءات فورية لحماية الحيوانات وسلامة الجمهور. يرجى تقديم تحديثات عامة حول القضية.

شكرًا لاهتمامكم بهذه المسألة العاجلة.

الأدلة: https://drive.google.com/file/d/17zqQ1qB7azuMl7tqwxXiLpkr_XhWEeG1/view?usp=sharing

مع خالص التقدير،

فريق @HallieForAnimals
contact@hallieforanimals.org



To whom it may concern,

I am writing to formally report a shocking incident of animal cruelty that occurred on Said Street, Army Square (Al-Daher area) in central Cairo. A man was captured on video kicking a helpless dog multiple times in broad daylight, leaving it terrified and unable to even look at its abuser. The dog was on a lead and collar, suggesting it may belong to the individual. Bystanders stood by without intervening, and the dog, already vulnerable, suffered pain and fear.

This act is not only a blatant violation of Egypt's laws but also directly threatens public health and safety.

Egypt faces a serious dog-related crisis, with the Ministry of Health reporting over 1.2 million animal bites or scratches from January to September 2025 alone (many dog-related), and figures reaching up to 1.4 million dog bite cases for the full year in some reports. Rabies remains a deadly risk, claiming an average of around 60 human lives annually despite post-exposure treatments being available. Dog populations (including strays and owned pets) drive these numbers, but unchecked abuse exacerbates the problem.

Such cruelty creates a dangerous cycle:
- Abusing a dog instills deep fear and trauma.
- A terrified, traumatized dog is far more likely to react defensively - snapping or biting when approached, cornered, or feeling threatened.
- This leads to more bites on people (especially children or unaware passersby).
- The dog then gets blamed, often euthanized or poisoned, while the root cause - abuse - goes unpunished.
- The cycle continues: more fear toward dogs, more cruelty, more aggression risks, and higher rabies exposure.

By failing to address this public abuse, our communities remain endangered. Holding the perpetrator accountable would deter future abuse, support Egypt's national rabies control efforts (including vaccinating and neutering thousands of strays toward the "Egypt Free of Rabies by 2030" goal), and reduce bite incidents for everyone.

Under Egypt's Penal Code (e.g., Articles 355-357), willful harm, killing, or cruelty to tame/domesticated animals is criminalized, with penalties including imprisonment and fines. The Public Prosecution has emphasized that animal abuse violates the law, religious values, and human ethics, urging investigations and strict action.

I urge you to:
- Identify and arrest the individual responsible.
- Conduct a thorough investigation using the provided evidence.
- Prosecute to the fullest extent of the law to set a public example.
- Coordinate with veterinary services for the dog's rescue/rehabilitation if possible.

This incident demands immediate action to protect both animals and public safety. Please provide public updates on the case.

Thank you for your attention to this urgent matter.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/17zqQ1qB7azuMl7tqwxXiLpkr_XhWEeG1/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Expel Animal Abuser Student From KITS
links.mailtoSubject: Full Investigation, Criminal Charges, and Permanent Expulsion for ALL Involved in KITS Kitten Cruelty Incident
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing as a deeply concerned citizen and advocate for animal welfare regarding the horrific animal cruelty incident at Karunya Institute of Technology and Sciences (KITS), Coimbatore, captured on video in March 2026.
A student deliberately threw a kitten from the third-floor balcony, causing grievous harm or death, while others present recorded it mockingly (e.g., with captions like "Helicopter helicopter"), laughed, and failed to intervene.

Public outrage has highlighted that this was a group act involving:  
- The primary perpetrator  
- At least one other individual visible in the background  
- The person recording/videoing for social media/"reels"  
- Additional bystanders who witnessed, encouraged, or did nothing to stop it

While a suspension memo (dated March 13, 2026) has been issued for the primary student, this interim measure is insufficient without escalation to permanent expulsion. All involved must face full accountability to reflect the severity of premeditated cruelty to a defenseless animal and to deter future incidents.

Under India's Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960 (Sections 11 for cruelty and abetment liability), and the university's code of conduct, I urgently request:

To the University Administration:  

1. Expand the internal investigation (using video, hostel records, witness statements) to identify and name all accomplices and enablers.  
2. Permanently expel every individual found involved (including those who recorded or failed to act) and bar them from future enrollment or campus access.  
3. Implement mandatory animal welfare, ethics, and empathy training for all students/hostel residents.  
4. Issue a public statement detailing actions against all parties and reaffirming zero tolerance for cruelty.

To the Coimbatore Police:  

1. Swiftly expand the existing FIR investigation to identify and charge all culpable individuals (perpetrator + accomplices/abetters).  
2. File a chargesheet promptly, pursuing maximum penalties (fines, imprisonment where evidence supports).  
3. Coordinate with KITS for evidence (e.g., video, student details) and provide public updates on progress.

Such collective cruelty demonstrates a profound failure of empathy and ethics - values central to any educational institution. Harsh, group-wide consequences are essential for justice, public trust, and protection of voiceless animals.

Thank you for your immediate action. Please issue updates on the investigation and steps taken.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1rF6o3OOnhjwks_EqGLR6que4OvTiTfqN/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

To whom it may concern,

This is a formal complaint/warning regarding a dog trainer operating in Seattle and Bellevue, WA.

Lamar of NightTimeK9

The allegations are time-critical because this involves ongoing handling practices that can cause immediate physical injury and long-term fear conditioning.

Observed conduct (demonstrated/promoted/used on dogs):
- hard lead yanks / leash pops with prong collars
- Ecollar use, adopting a pain-based training tecnique.

We demand:

1) Credential/status check:
Determine whether Lamar of NightTimeK9 is currently (or has ever been) certified, credentialed, listed, endorsed, or a member under your organization.

2) Immediate enforcement where applicable:
If he is connected to your organization in any capacity, treat this as a potential ethics/standards violation and take action consistent with your rules (investigation, suspension/revocation, removal from directories, disciplinary record, etc.).

3) Public clarification (where applicable):
If your organization is not associated with him, or if he has been removed/dropped, publish a clear public statement clarifying that he is not certified/listed/member (or that his status has been revoked/ended), so your name cannot be used, directly or indirectly, to imply legitimacy.

4) Clear routing / no quiet closures:
Do not bounce this between departments or silently close it out. If your org is not the correct venue for action, route internally to the correct ethics/standards contact and log this as a formal warning.

Because abusive trainers often continue operating behind implied credentials and brand association, this notice is being sent across multiple trainer bodies/certification orgs.

links.mailtoSubject: Neglect At 417 Aldis St, Los Angeles
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

This is a formal report of severe, ongoing animal cruelty and neglect at 1417 Aldis St, Los Angeles, CA 90001.

Two adult dogs (one loose and distressed in the front area; one confined in the backyard).
One black kitten (frequently roaming unattended).

Observed Conditions:

Extended owner absences (days at a time) with no access to food or water provided.
Severe emaciation in both dogs (ribs, spine, and hip bones prominently visible), indicating starvation and dehydration.
Physical abuse including beating the dogs, tying them together as punishment, and throwing one dog forcefully against concrete, causing limping and disorientation.
One dog repeatedly climbing onto the roof, posing imminent risk of fall and injury/death.
Explicit prohibition to neighbors against providing food or water, with stated intent to "train" the animals as "savages" that must hunt to survive.
Multiple prior animals on the property that have disappeared over the past year.

Requested Actions:
Immediate welfare check, assessment, and removal/rescue of the animals by DACC and LASD to prevent further suffering or death.
Coordinated criminal investigation by LASD and DACC into the neglect, abuse, and potential prior incidents.
Public statement from relevant agencies confirming steps taken or planned, and updating the community on the status of intervention.

Public Safety Link: Animal Cruelty as Predictor of Human Violence:
Animal cruelty is a well-documented predictor and co-occurring factor with violence against humans. The FBI tracks animal cruelty offenses in its National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS) precisely because of this link, recognizing it as associated with assault, domestic violence, rape, murder, arson, and child abuse. Landmark research, including a Massachusetts SPCA/Northeastern University study, shows animal abusers are five times more likely to commit violent crimes against people. Additional studies indicate animal abuse occurs in up to 88% of homes investigated for child physical abuse, underscoring the broader community risk and the need for swift enforcement.

Relevant California Penal Code Violations (Based on Described Conduct):
Penal Code § 597(a): Maliciously and intentionally maims, mutilates, tortures, wounds, or kills a living animal.
Penal Code § 597(b): Overworks, tortures, torments, deprives of necessary sustenance/drink/shelter, cruelly beats, mutilates, or cruelly kills an animal; or, as owner/custodian, subjects an animal to needless suffering, inflicts unnecessary cruelty, abuses an animal, or fails to provide proper food, drink, shelter, or protection from the weather.

A prior report has been submitted to DACC. This escalates the matter for coordinated response due to the severity, ongoing nature, and public safety implications.
Please provide a public statement on actions taken or in progress.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1os9qx_e7R6RKTUegTtIfVo1Y4ARCky63/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Ban Lee Cox From Showing Dogs
links.descriptionLong:
Abuser Name: Lee Cox

This year's winner of Best in Show at Crufts, Lee Cox, was convicted of animal cruelty in 2001 for causing unnecessary suffering to Adam the cocker spaniel, who suffered a chronic ear infection, ulcerated/oozing pus, and requiring inner ear amputation in disgusting conditions at Kaston Kennels.
He was sentenced to three years' conditional discharge and ordered to pay £5,000 in costs to the RSPCA.
No ban on keeping dogs was imposed.
The Royal Kennel Club knew he was a convicted animal abuser, but said: "It was an isolated incident with no issues since."
No one would allow a child abuser to enter a child into a beauty pageant, so why was Cox allowed to show at Crufts?
links.mailtoSubject: Ban Lee Cox From Showing Dogs
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

This year's Crufts Best in Show winning handler Lee Cox was convicted in 2001 under the Protection of Animals Act 1911 for causing unnecessary suffering to a cocker spaniel named Adam in "disgusting" kennel conditions at Kaston Kennels (co-run by Cox). The dog suffered a chronic ear infection that ulcerated, oozed pus, and required surgical removal of the inner ear.  

At no point would someone convicted of child abuse be allowed to enter a child into a beauty pageant, so why is a convicted animal abuser permitted to handle, show, or judge dogs at the world's largest dog event? Your "isolated incident" claim ignores documented risks:  

- 96% of animal abusers have prior or subsequent criminal records (41% interpersonal violence, 18% sex offenses).  
- Near 100% recidivism in cases like animal hoarding.  
- 85% of animal cruelty arrests involve multiple prior arrests (70% felonies).  
- 70% of domestic violence perpetrators are also animal abusers.  

Animal abuse correlates strongly with violent and sexual offenses, abusers are up to 5 times more likely to commit crimes against humans, escalating from animals to assault, rape, domestic violence, or worse. Allowing Cox access undermines animal welfare, endangers dogs, and erodes public trust.  

We demand:  
- Immediate permanent ban on Lee Cox from all Kennel Club/Crufts events, handling, judging, exhibiting, breeding privileges, or affiliated activities.  
- Review/update of eligibility criteria for convicted animal abusers.  
- Public statement addressing the conviction and actions taken.  

RSPCA: Investigate current welfare at Cox's facilities.  

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.mailtoSubject: Report of Animal Cruelty by Dog Trainer Ashleah Suzanne (Smart Dogs Walk)
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am reporting suspected animal cruelty by Ashleah Suzanne Chiacchiero , owner/operator of Smart Dogs Walk (813 Snowden Dr, Lake Worth Beach, FL 33461; Website: www.smartdogswalk.com Instagram: @smartdogswalk). She promotes and uses prong collars and e-collars (shock collars) as training tools, including on a 6lb puppy. These devices inflict unnecessary pain and suffering by design, violating Florida Statute 828.12(1) (misdemeanor cruelty for unnecessary torment) and potentially 828.12(2) (third-degree felony aggravated cruelty for intentional acts causing excessive or repeated unnecessary pain). Palm Beach County Code Sec. 4-24 also prohibits physical abuse of animals.

Veterinary consensus (American Veterinary Society of Animal Behavior position statement) rejects prong and e-collars due to risks of neck trauma (tracheal collapse, thyroid damage, cervical spine injury), fear-based aggression, and elevated stress (cortisol spikes). Studies link these aversive tools to increased aggression:

* Herron et al. (2009, Applied Animal Behaviour Science): Prong collars elicit aggressive responses (growling, snapping, biting) in 25-43% of dogs.
* Schalke et al. (2007) and Cooper et al. (2014, PLoS ONE): E-collars cause severe stress, fear, and aggression as escape responses, with no long-term efficacy over positive methods.
* Vieira de Castro et al. (2020, PLoS ONE): Aversive tools correlate with higher fear-based behaviors and aggression versus reward-based training.

This pattern of inflicting avoidable pain on a defenseless small animal signals empathy deficits and desensitization to suffering. It is a documented red flag for broader violence per FBI research on "The Link": Animal abusers are 5 times more likely to commit violent crimes (assault, domestic abuse) and 3 times more likely for sexual offenses.
Request:

* PBSO Animal Cruelty Unit: Immediate investigation, including review of her business practices, social media promotions, and any client/subject animals for patterns of abuse. Seize affected animals if ongoing harm is evident.
* State Attorney's Office: Review for prosecution under FS 828.12(1)/(2) upon investigation completion.


Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/11QovWQSkuzom-4mkRor7Xh2O-CgFFhJY/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.mailtoSubject: Prosecute Deepika Patidar & Pooja Panchal
links.mailtoBody:
जिस किसी को भी यह मामला संबंधित हो,

रतलाम के लालबाग एवेन्यू (बास्केटबॉल कोर्ट के पास) का एक वायरल वीडियो दिखाता है कि दीपिका पटेल (1392 लालबाग एवेन्यू) और पूजा पंचाल (1390 लालबाग एवेन्यू) एक सड़क पर पड़े पिल्ले को स्कूटर से बांधकर सड़क पर घसीट रही हैं, जिससे उसे गंभीर चोटें और अनावश्यक पीड़ा हो रही है।

मीडिया/जन शिकायतों के बाद प्राथमिकी दर्ज की गई (इंडस्ट्रियल एरिया पुलिस स्टेशन, 6/7 मार्च, 2026)। यह **पशु क्रूरता निवारण अधिनियम, 1960, धारा 11(1)(ए)** के तहत स्पष्ट रूप से **क्रूरता** है: पीटना, लात मारना, ऊपर से चढ़ना, या अन्यथा अनावश्यक दर्द/पीड़ा पहुँचाना (जिसमें जुर्माना/कारावास के साथ संज्ञेय अपराध है)।

पशु क्रूरता मनुष्यों के खिलाफ हिंसक और यौन अपराधों का एक सिद्ध संकेतक और भविष्यवाणी है (व्यापक शोध द्वारा समर्थित, जिसमें एफबीआई के व्यवहारिक अध्ययन शामिल हैं जो पशु दुर्व्यवहार को पारस्परिक हिंसा, घरेलू दुर्व्यवहार और यौन अपराधों के उच्च जोखिम से जोड़ते हैं)।

हम तत्काल मांग करते हैं:
1. पीसीए अधिनियम की धारा 11 के तहत आरोपी को गिरफ्तार किया जाए और उन पर औपचारिक आरोप लगाए जाएं।
2. उनके कब्जे से किसी भी जानवर को जब्त किया जाए।
3. कल्याण और अभियोजन के लिए पशुपालन/AWBI के साथ समन्वय।
4. जांच और की गई कार्रवाइयों पर सार्वजनिक अपडेट।

कार्रवाई में विफलता हिंसा को सामान्य बनाती है।

सबूत: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1XCXnHOCr68vOq5rjrCuQkRRRi1GHMUsw/view?usp=sharing

सादर,

@HallieForAnimals टीम
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.mailtoSubject: Demand Accountability For Bahamas Officer
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

Disturbing video footage shows a Royal Bahamas Police Force officer shooting a family-owned dog on the roadside in Nassau. The dog was not attacking anyone and posed no imminent threat.

This act constitutes potential cruelty under the Animal Protection and Control Act 2010 (Ch. 378):
- Cruelty is defined as any act causing unnecessary or unjustifiable pain/suffering (Section 2).
- Prohibited acts include cruelly beating, injuring, abusing, or inhumanely killing an animal (related provisions under repealed Penal Code sections now consolidated here, with penalties in Second Schedule including fines up to $500+ for abuse/neglect/mistreatment).

The Bahamas cannot normalize violence against animals by law enforcement. Animal cruelty is a recognized predictor and indicator of future violent and sexual offenses against people (supported by decades of criminological research, including links used in offender profiling).

We demand:
1. Immediate suspension of the involved officer pending full independent investigation.
2. Transparent public report on the incident, including body cam footage (if available) and justification under use-of-force policy.
3. Mandatory humane training for all RBPF officers on animal encounters, de-escalation, and non-lethal options.
4. Prosecution if evidence shows unnecessary force or cruelty under Ch. 378.

Failure to act erodes public trust and signals tolerance for violence.

This incident highlights the urgent need for stronger enforcement of animal protection laws and links to human safety.

Thank you for your prompt action.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1aV2ZxmfEjYTqSCon-TOx1m3pk-c6huJI/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.mailtoSubject: Підозра у жорстокому поводженні з тваринами за адресою: Небесна Сотня, 5, Тайрова, Одеса/Suspected criminal animal crueltyat Nebesnoi Sotni 5, Tairova, Odesa
links.mailtoBody:
До відома всіх, кого це стосується,

Я вимагаю негайного втручання у явний випадок жорстокого поводження з тваринами за адресою Небесна Сотня, 5, Тайрова дільниця, Одеса.
Згідно з повідомленнями свідків, собака (можливо, такса) утримується в умовах крайнього нехтування: скелетоподібне виснаження, ледь здатна рухатися, відкриті рани на лапах. Власник відреагував агресивно та ірраціонально, коли його викрили. Це серйозні, небезпечні для життя страждання, які за українським законодавством кваліфікуються як жорстоке поводження.

Згідно зі статтею 299 Кримінального кодексу України (Жорстоке поводження з тваринами), жорстоке поводження з хребетними тваринами (включаючи заподіяння тривалих страждань, травм або умов, що призводять до каліцтва/смерті) карається штрафом або позбавленням волі на строк до 6 місяців (або більше в особливо тяжких випадках).
Закон України «Про захист тварин від жорстокого поводження» (№ 3447-IV) підкреслює, що власники повинні забезпечувати гуманні умови; невиконання цього обов'язку, що призводить до травм або страждань, тягне за собою кримінальну/адміністративну відповідальність.
Собака, зведена до кісток з ранами на лапах, є незаперечним доказом злочину, а не «сірою зоною».
Жорстоке поводження з тваринами є доведеним раннім показником насильницьких/сексуальних злочинів проти людей. Ігнорування цього факту загрожує подальшою ескалацією.
Вимагайте вжиття термінових заходів:

1. Негайну перевірку добробуту тварин на місці за адресою: Небесна Сотня, 5, Тайрова, Одеса.
2. Негайне ветеринарне обстеження всіх тварин (за необхідності залучити Державну службу України з питань безпеки харчових продуктів та захисту споживачів).
3. Вилучення/захист собаки (собак) у разі підтвердження недбальства/жорстокого поводження, не повертати їх власнику.
4. Порушити кримінальну справу за статтею 299 КК України (та відповідними статтями, якщо це доречно).
5. Провести повне розслідування, щоб запобігти повторенню та захистити громадську безпеку.

Час має вирішальне значення, тварина може скоро померти без втручання. Дійте негайно.

Докази: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1U-wuxFaQvESrK6LV1BwNFNZ_GI0DRnJu/view?usp=sharing

З повагою,

Команда @HallieForAnimals

links.mailtoSubject: Demand full prosecution for the deliberate killing of two puppies in Tiruchirappalli
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing to demand immediate and rigorous prosecution in the animal cruelty case against **J. Meenakshi Mishra** of Kambarasampettai, near Tiruchirappalli (Trichy), Tamil Nadu.

On March 4, 2026, J. Meenakshi Mishra deliberately grabbed two newborn stray puppies and slammed them forcefully against a wall, killing them instantly. CCTV footage of this brutal act has circulated widely, showing clear intent to inflict extreme violence on defenceless animals out of rage stemming from a neighbour dispute. This was premeditated cruelty to living beings used as outlets for personal anger.

Such acts constitute serious offences under Indian law:

- **Section 11** of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960 (PCA Act), which prohibits treating animals cruelly by beating, torturing, or otherwise subjecting them to unnecessary pain or suffering. Penalties include fines and up to three months' imprisonment for repeat or aggravated cases.
- **Section 428** of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) (or equivalent under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita if applicable post-2023 reforms), punishing mischief by killing, maiming, or injuring any animal of value ₹10 or more, with up to two years' imprisonment, fine, or both. This is cognizable and bailable but demands serious handling.
- **Section 429** IPC/BNS for more severe cases involving killing or rendering useless animals, carrying up to five years' imprisonment.

Research consistently links animal cruelty to broader violent behaviour: those who torture or kill animals often escalate to harming humans, including domestic violence, child abuse, or sexual offences. Allowing this case to be downplayed as a "neighbour spat" or emotional outburst ignores this established pattern and risks public safety.

I urge your office to:

- File and pursue the strongest possible charges under the PCA Act Sections 11, and IPC/BNS Sections 428/429 (or equivalents).
- Treat this as deliberate aggravated cruelty, not negligence or a trivial dispute.
- Oppose any bail, compounding, or summary disposal that avoids full trial and accountability.
- Seek maximum penalties, including imprisonment, to reflect the brutality and serve as a deterrent.
- Ensure transparent investigation, no undue leniency, and public updates on progress.

The public is outraged, as seen in widespread social media and news coverage. Weak action here signals that torturing and killing animals carries negligible consequences, undermining animal welfare laws and public trust in the justice system.

Do your duty: prosecute J. Meenakshi Mishra fully and vigorously under the law.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vXC2aCWlt-BNoRY44p1dUwF9yyLa9VEW/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.mailtoSubject: Demand Felony Prosecution in Startburst German Shepherd Cruelty Case
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I'm writing regarding the abandonment and abuse of a 5-month-old German Shepherd named Startburst, who was dumped outside in a covered crate exposed to the elements. The dog's collar had a QR code linking to the owner at 7616 Pacific Blvd #319, Huntington Park, CA 90255. When confronted, the owner initially denied ownership but then admitted, "F**k that b***h dog, she bit me." The dog has both new and old wounds, indicating long-term abuse despite her young age. Huntington Park Police are already investigating this case.

As you are aware, this constitutes felony animal cruelty under California Penal Code Section 597(a), which prohibits maliciously maiming, mutilating, torturing, or wounding a living animal, punishable by up to 3 years in state prison and/or a $20,000 fine. It also violates Section 597(b), covering the willful infliction of unnecessary suffering, including deprivation of necessary shelter or protection from weather, with the same felony penalties. The abandonment aspect falls under Section 597f, which can be charged as a misdemeanor or felony.

Animal abuse like this is a proven predictor of violence against humans. FBI research and multiple studies show that animal abusers are up to five times more likely to commit violent crimes against people, including domestic violence, child abuse, sexual offenses, assault, rape, and homicide. This "Link" between animal cruelty and violent/sexual offenses desensitizes perpetrators and often escalates to broader threats against the community, we cannot ignore it.

We expect the full force of the law to come down hard on this abuser: no excuses, no leniency, no plea deals that minimize accountability.

- File felony charges under Penal Code Sections 597(a), 597(b), and 597f with no reductions to misdemeanors.
- Seek maximum penalties, including up to 3 years in state prison per count, $20,000 fines per count, and full restitution for the dog's veterinary care and any ongoing needs.
- Reject any plea agreements that reduce charges or penalties.
- Issue a public statement from your offices on the charges filed, the prosecution status, and the established link between animal cruelty and human violence to deter similar crimes and protect the community.
- Provide public statement with updates on the investigation status, charges filed (or referral to the DA), and next steps.

This abuser must face serious consequences to protect animals and people alike.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1oP5pRjaalHxVHjNKhRKcxxlek9WHSdYK/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

To whom it may concern,

A dog kept at 9016 Peachtree St, Houston, TX 77016 (owned by Charles Douglas, white 2022 Ford F-250, Texas plate 1555985) is still tethered outside 24/7 with no adequate shelter, no food, and no water. The animal is severely emaciated - ribs and spine clearly protruding. Animal control has visited multiple times and issued citations with zero improvement. No criminal charges have been filed despite repeat violations. Houston SPCA has been notified with no visible progress.

This is ongoing neglect in violation of:
- Texas Penal Code Sec. 42.092(b)(3) and (b)(6): unjustifiably failing to provide necessary food, water, or care/shelter (Class A misdemeanor, escalates to state jail felony on repeat or if serious bodily injury results).
- Texas Health & Safety Code Sec. 821.101 et seq. (Safe Outdoor Dogs Act): prohibits continuous tethering without access to drinkable water, adequate shade/shelter, and prohibits tethering that injures or endangers the animal.

Animal neglect/abuse is a proven gateway to human violence. FBI studies show animal abusers are 5x more likely to commit violent crimes (assault, domestic violence, sexual offenses) against people. Continued inaction here risks escalation beyond the animal.

Demanded Actions:
- Seize the dog immediately under Health & Safety Code Sec. 821.022 for emergency impoundment due to cruel treatment.
- Arrest Charles Douglas for animal cruelty under Penal Code Sec. 42.092.
- File charges promptly - no more citations without teeth.
- Provide a public statement with steps taken, including seizure date/time and any arrests/charges filed.

links.title: Stop The Iditarod Race
links.descriptionLong:
The Iditarod starts again on March 7th.

In 2025 four year old Ventana died on the trail, 300 miles into the race. Necropsy revealed she was pregnant, which was conveniently missed in pre-race vet checks and oversight.
links.mailtoSubject: How Can We Call Ourselves "Land of the Free" While Iditarod Kills Dogs for Profit?
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

How can America call herself the "land of the free" when the Iditarod Trail Sled Dog Race profits from chaining dogs to endless suffering, and forcing them to run until they collapse and die, all shielded by law from real cruelty charges?

Freedom? No. It's exploitation. Dogs don't consent to 1000+ mile grueling runs in brutal cold for human prizes and sponsorship money. Yet Alaska Statute 11.61.140(e) gives a free pass: "This section does not apply to generally accepted dog mushing or pulling contests or practices." That loophole means preventable deaths aren't treated as cruelty here.

Take Ventana: In the 2025 Iditarod, this 4-year-old dog collapsed and died on the trail about 8 miles outside Galena after running over 300 miles for rookie musher Daniel Klein. Necropsy confirmed she was pregnant in a later stage, her condition missed in pre-race vet checks and oversight. She and her unborn puppies died. No criminal charges followed, despite calls for investigation. The musher was later disqualified by the Iditarod committee for inadequate care, but that's internal not justice under state law.

528 dogs started in 2025, 314 dropped for injury, exhaustion, or illness, only 214 finished. Over 150 dogs have died in Iditarod history from pneumonia, heart failure, ulcers, collapse, all pushed too far.

You hold the power, budgets, and laws. Millions in sponsorships and support flow while dogs pay with their lives. When deaths happen and nothing changes, it exposes the hypocrisy: boasting freedom while denying it to sentient animals for "tradition" and profit.

We demand:

1. Cancel all future Iditarod races immediately (ceremonial start is days away on March 7, 2026, stop it before more suffer) and publicly support ending the event.
2. Repeal the mushing exemption in AS 11.61.140(e) through emergency legislation so cruelty laws apply fully, no more shields for deadly contests.
3. Launch an independent criminal investigation into Ventana's 2025 death and other fatalities/drops, referring for charges where neglect or overexertion shows.
4. Require transparent public reports on every death, drop reason, necropsy, and actions taken.
5. Condemn this exploitation on your platforms and commit to real change, no more silence or defense.

Dogs deserve freedom from pain, fear, and exploitation, the same ideals we claim as a nation. Ventana's preventable death proves the system fails them.

Act now. Deliver results before the 2026 race starts. We expect concrete steps and public responses.

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Demand Change At LA Animal Services
links.descriptionLong:
LA Animal Services has been in the spotlight too often and for too long.

Romeo (#A2256618) is available for adoption at South LA Shelter.
links.mailtoSubject: We The People Demand Change
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

LA Animal Services has been under the spotlight for far too long and far too often. It’s past time for leadership to treat this as the serious public trust issue that it is.

A recently shared video shows a South LA/Chesterfield Square shelter employee forcing a dog into a transport kennel barely large enough for the dog to turn around. The employee uses his foot to force the dog in, closes the door on the dog’s legs, and continues handling the animal in a way no reasonable person would call “humane.” That is cruelty, and it creates an unacceptable risk of injury.

We are not just disgruntled residents. We are tax-paying citizens who do not appreciate our hard-earned tax dollars funding the cruel treatment of some of the most vulnerable residents of Los Angeles. Just because dogs can’t pay taxes does not mean they don’t deserve fair treatment and a fair chance at life. Lest you forget, that we are also the voters keeping you in, or as they case may be without systematic change, out of office.

WHAT MUST CHANGE

1. Locate and identify the employee in the attached video and remove them from active duty immediately.

2. A zero-tolerance handling standard for transport/loading that explicitly prohibits:

   * using feet to force animals into kennels,
   * forcing animals into kennels too small to safely fit,
   * closing kennel doors on limbs, tails, or paws,
   * "shove, slam, and pray" handling under any circumstances.

3. Written, public kennel sizing and transport standards:

   * clear minimum dimensions by weight/size,
   * "fit checks" supervisors must enforce,
   * required alternatives when correct kennels are not available.

4. Enforcement that actually means something:

   * immediate removal from animal-handling duties for staff who violate these standards,
   * documented discipline (up to and including termination),
   * mandatory retraining for anyone handling transport kennels.

5. Public accountability:

   * publish the updated policy/SOP and training requirements,
   * publish monthly metrics on complaints and outcomes (investigations opened/closed, discipline imposed, retraining completed).

The public should not have to play "catch it on video" to get humane handling in a city shelter. This is a baseline expectation, not a special request.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1hRWIf3D4EWUd6q_tEX64qoke0aaKTha_/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Arrest Uzbek Dog Skinners
links.descriptionLong:
Location: Angren, Tashkent, Uzbekistan

Government workers for the "animal catching service" were caught by volunteers skinning a dog.
They're allegedly supplying various canteens and cafes with dog meat.
links.mailtoSubject: Арестовать узбекских собачников/O'zbek it terisini yechuvchilarni hibsga oling/Arrest Uzbek Dog Skinners
links.mailtoBody:
Кому это может быть интересно,

На видео из Ангрена (Ташкентская область) видно, как мужчины, предположительно связанные с муниципальной службой отлова животных («отлов»), снимают шкуру с мертвой собаки.

Это недопустимо. Требуется немедленное расследование и привлечение к ответственности — как за то, что показано на видео, так и за вопросы, на которые оно не дает ответов: откуда взялась собака, как она умерла, кто санкционировал это и не происходит ли незаконная обработка или распространение тел/частей тел животных.

ТРЕБУЕМЫЕ ДЕЙСТВИЯ:
1) Установить личности всех причастных лиц (имена, должности, работодатель/подрядчик).
2) Немедленно отстранить их от любых обязанностей, связанных с обращением с животными, на время расследования.
3) Начать официальное расследование инцидента, включая происхождение собаки и обстоятельства ее смерти.
4) Провести аудит записей/журналов отлова и утилизации, договоров с подрядчиками и мест утилизации, связанных со службой отлова.
5) Провести ветеринарно-санитарную и пищевую инспекцию, чтобы подтвердить отсутствие незаконной переработки, хранения, передачи или поставки в кафе/столовые/рестораны.
6) Применить максимальные меры наказания, предусмотренные законодательством Узбекистана, ко всем ответственным лицам, включая руководителей, если будет установлена халатность или сокрытие фактов.

Власти должны обеспечить привлечение виновных к ответственности и недопущение повторения подобных случаев.

Доказательства: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1EdkEUEQIK_wxJb3TQTirjg-bV9ze_p8h/view?usp=sharing

С уважением,

Команда @HallieForAnimals
contact@hallieforanimals.org



Qiziqqan har kimga,

Angren (Toshkent viloyati)dan olingan videoda shahar hayvonlarni ushlash xizmati ("otlov")ga aloqadorligi aytilayotgan erkaklar o'lik itni terisini yechayotgani ko'rinadi.

Bu qabul qilib bo'lmas holatdir. Bu darhol tergov o'tkazish va javobgarlikni ta'minlashni talab qiladi – videoda ko'rsatilgan voqealar uchun ham, shuningdek quyidagi savollarga javob topish uchun ham: it qayerdan kelgan, qanday o'lgan, buni kim ruxsat bergan va hayvon jasadlari yoki ularning qismlari noqonuniy ravishda qayta ishlanayotgani yoki tarqatilayotgani haqida.

KO'RILISHI TALAB QILINAYOTGAN CHORA-TADBIRLAR:
1) Hodisada ishtirok etgan barcha shaxslarni aniqlash (famelari, lavozimi, ish beruvchi/pudratchi).
2) Tergov davom etayotgan paytda ularni hayvonlar bilan ishlash bo'yicha vazifalardan darhol ozod qilish.
3) Hodisa bo'yicha, jumladan itning kelib chiqishi va o'lim sharoitlarini o'rganish uchun rasmiy tergov boshlash.
4) Hayvonlarni ushlash va utilizatsiya qilish yozuvlari/jurnalini, pudratchi bilan tuzilgan shartnomalarni hamda ushlash xizmatiga tegishli utilizatsiya joylarini audit qilish.
5) Qonunga zid qayta ishlash, saqlash, uzatish yoki kafelar/kantinalar/restoranlarga yetkazib berilmasligini tasdiqlash uchun veterinar-sanitariya va oziq-ovqat xavfsizligi bo'yicha tekshiruv o'tkazish.
6) Agar beparvolik yoki yashirish aniqlansa, rahbarlar hamda barcha mas'ul shaxslarga nisbatan O'zbekiston qonunchiligida nazarda tutilgan eng qat'iy choralarni qo'llash.

Hokimiyat organlari mas'ullar javobgarlikka tortilishini va bunday holatning takrorlanmasligini ta'minlashi kerak.

Dalillar: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1EdkEUEQIK_wxJb3TQTirjg-bV9ze_p8h/view?usp=sharing

Hurmat bilan,

@HallieForAnimals jamoasi
contact@hallieforanimals.org




To whom it may concern,

A video from Angren (Tashkent Region) shows men reportedly connected to the municipal animal capture service ("otlov") skinning a dead dog.

This is unacceptable. It demands immediate investigation and accountability - both for what is shown in the video and for the unanswered questions it raises: where the dog came from, how it died, who authorized this, and whether animal bodies/parts are being unlawfully handled or distributed.

REQUESTED ACTIONS:
1) Identify every person involved (names, positions, employer/contractor).
2) Remove them from any animal-handling duties immediately while the investigation is underway.
3) Open a formal investigation into the incident, including the origin of the dog and the circumstances of death.
4) Audit capture and disposal records/logs, contractor arrangements, and disposal locations tied to the capture service.
5) Conduct a veterinary-sanitary and food safety inspection to confirm no unlawful processing, storage, transfer, or supply to any cafes/canteens/restaurants.
6) Apply the maximum enforcement measures available under Uzbek law against all responsible parties, including supervisors if negligence or cover-up is found.

Authorities must ensure those responsible are held accountable and that this does not happen again.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1EdkEUEQIK_wxJb3TQTirjg-bV9ze_p8h/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Arrest Brooklyn Dog Abusers
links.descriptionLong:
Date & Time: February 28, :35
Location: Morgan Ave & Johnson Ave, Brooklyn, NY

The cops were called, they did nothing. A squad car was flagged down two blocks away, they did nothing too.
links.mailtoSubject: Animal cruelty on video + reported NYPD non-response
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am reporting animal cruelty captured on video at/near Morgan Ave and Johnson Ave in Brooklyn on February 28, 2026.

In the footage, two individuals (one male, one female) are seen kicking and punching a dog and aggressively yanking the dog by the leash. When confronted, they respond with an “it’s my dog/none of your business” attitude.

Date & Time: February 28, 2026 - 18:35
Location: Morgan Ave & Johnson Ave, Brooklyn, NY

The person who recorded the video states they called the police at the time, and that responding officers did not take action. They also state they flagged down a marked NYPD squad car a couple of blocks away and that officers again did not take action.

Requested Actions:

1. Open an animal cruelty investigation immediately and assign it a case number.
2. Identify both individuals using the video and nearby CCTV (business/building cameras, traffic cameras, etc.).
3. Conduct a welfare check and ensure the dog is safe. If probable cause exists, remove the dog from the abuser(s).
4. Document injuries and pursue appropriate charges.
5. Provide a public statement including the complaint/case number for follow-up.
6. Separately: Please review the reported non-response by officers/squad car as described above and confirm how this will be addressed internally (e.g., supervisor review, memo book/body-worn camera review if applicable).

I am asking for enforcement and documentation. This is violence and the dog may still be in danger.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1rrohu3vmNSKvfYCXuukaCS86Uo5IES4R/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Prosecute Il Tacano Pet Groomer
links.descriptionLong:
Location: Il Tucano Pet, Via dei Castelli Romani, 55, 00071 Pomezia (RM), Lazio, Italy

This POS was fired, but so far, there's no confirmation of an arrest.
links.mailtoSubject: Pomezia: richiesta di indagine formale e pena massima/Pomezia: request for formal investigation and maximum penalty
links.mailtoBody:
Alla cortese attenzione,

Vi scrivo in merito al video che mostra un toelettatore aggredire fisicamente un cane durante una sessione di toelettatura presso/associata a “Il Tucano Pet” (Pomezia).

Per chiarezza: sono consapevole che il filmato è già ampiamente diffuso, che il cane è stato dichiarato in salvo e che è stato riferito che il dipendente coinvolto è stato licenziato. Questi fatti non risolvono la questione. Il “licenziamento” non è giustizia e non impedisce che il reato venga commesso altrove.

È necessaria un'indagine penale formale e un procedimento giudiziario con le massime sanzioni applicabili.

Azioni richieste:

1. INDAGINE FORMALE E IDENTIFICAZIONE

* Confermare che sia stato aperto un caso ufficiale per maltrattamento di animali e identificare l'ufficio investigativo che se ne occupa.
* Identificare la persona ripresa nel filmato con piena attribuzione legale (non solo “è stato licenziato”), compresa la verifica dell'identità e del ruolo ricoperto nell'azienda al momento dei fatti.

2. ACQUISIZIONE E CONSERVAZIONE DELLE PROVE

* Mettere al sicuro i file originali del video, i metadati disponibili e ottenere tutte le registrazioni delle telecamere a circuito chiuso e i registri aziendali pertinenti (turni del personale, registri degli appuntamenti, ricevute, tesserini identificativi del personale).
* Preservare la catena di custodia in modo che il caso non venga compromesso in seguito da lacune procedurali evitabili.

3. AMBITO: VERIFICARE LA PRESENZA DI ALTRE VITTIME

* Determinare se si è trattato di un incidente isolato o se rientra in un quadro più ampio. Intervistare il personale, esaminare eventuali filmati aggiuntivi disponibili e invitare testimoni/clienti a farsi avanti.

4. AZIONE PENALE E SANZIONI MASSIME

* Perseguire le accuse più gravi applicabili e le pene massime previste dalla legge italiana.
* Ove legalmente possibile, richiedere restrizioni/divieti per impedire a questa persona di lavorare con animali in qualsiasi veste, in attesa dell'esito del procedimento e anche successivamente.

5. RESPONSABILITÀ PUBBLICA

* Confermare pubblicamente che il caso è aperto e che sono state raccolte le prove, includendo un numero di riferimento, se possibile.

Il pubblico ha già fatto la parte più difficile: le prove esistono, l'incidente è documentato e, secondo quanto riferito, l'accesso dell'individuo agli animali in quel luogo di lavoro è stato interrotto. Ora le istituzioni devono fare la loro parte: indagare, perseguire e imporre conseguenze proporzionate al reato.

Prove: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1qCQrP3Vkm-m18hPeNwoGqrHoPyDSEUyp/view?usp=sharing

Cordiali saluti,

Il team di @HallieForAnimals
contact@hallieforanimals.org



To whom it may concern,

I am writing regarding the video showing a groomer physically assaulting a dog during a grooming session at/associated with “Il Tucano Pet” (Pomezia).

To be clear: I am aware the footage is already widely circulating, the dog is confirmed to be safe, and it has been reported that the employee involved has been dismissed. Those facts do not resolve this matter. “Fired” is not justice, and it does not prevent reoffending elsewhere.

This requires a formal criminal investigation and prosecution with maximum applicable penalties.

Requested actions:

1. FORMAL INVESTIGATION AND IDENTIFICATION

* Confirm an official case has been opened for animal cruelty and identify the investigating office handling it.
* Identify the individual in the footage with full legal attribution (not just “he was fired”), including verification of identity and role at the business at the time.

2. EVIDENCE ACQUISITION AND PRESERVATION

* Secure the original file(s) of the video, metadata where available, and obtain any relevant CCTV and business records (staff rosters, appointment logs, receipts, staff IDs).
* Preserve chain-of-custody so the case is not undermined later by avoidable procedural gaps.

3. SCOPE: CHECK FOR OTHER VICTIMS

* Determine whether this was an isolated incident or part of a pattern. Interview staff, review additional footage where available, and invite witnesses/clients to come forward.

4. PROSECUTION AND MAXIMUM SANCTIONS

* Pursue the strongest applicable charges and maximum penalties available under Italian law.
* Where legally possible, seek restrictions/prohibitions to prevent this individual from working with animals in any capacity pending outcome and thereafter.

5. PUBLIC ACCOUNTABILITY

* Provide a public confirmation that the case is active and that evidence has been secured, including a reference number if one can be shared.

The public has already done the hard part: the evidence exists, the incident is documented, and the individual’s access to animals at that workplace has reportedly ended. Now the institutions must do theirs: investigate, prosecute, and impose consequences proportionate to the act.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1qCQrP3Vkm-m18hPeNwoGqrHoPyDSEUyp/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org
