links.mailtoSubject: Welfare check requested - dog kept outside 24/7
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am requesting an immediate animal welfare check at:

25208 SW 133rd Pl, Homestead, FL 33032

A dog is reportedly being kept outdoors 24/7. From what can be observed, there is NO visible food or water available for the dog, and there is NO adequate shelter (no proper cover from heat/rain and no safe, protective housing).

It has also been alleged that the dog is being left outside because another dog inside the home is pregnant. Regardless of the reason, the dog outdoors must have adequate shelter, shade, and continuous access to clean water and appropriate care.

This is a welfare concern that needs an in-person check, documentation, and enforcement action as appropriate. Florida conditions can be dangerous quickly without shade, water, and shelter.

Observed/Reported issues:

* Dog kept outside at all hours (24/7)
* Food and water cannot be seen out for the dog
* No adequate shelter visible
* Allegedly left outside due to another dog inside being pregnant

Request:

* Please dispatch the appropriate officer/inspector to perform a welfare check
* Verify access to clean water, food, adequate shelter/shade, and overall condition
* Document findings and take enforcement action if standards are not met
* Provide a public statement confirming findings

links.title: Remove Dog From Abusive Owner in Bogota
links.descriptionLong:
The (in)competent authorities have assessed this situation and decided there's nothing they can do because the owner is autistic...
You would not leave a child in an abusive household simply because a parent has a disability. The same should apply to a dog.
links.mailtoSubject: Engativa (Altos de Granada) - Remove The Abused Dog Now
links.mailtoBody:
A quien corresponda:

Esto se refiere al reciente caso de maltrato animal en Engativa, Bogotá (complejo residencial Altos de Granada), donde, según se informa, un perro fue agredido físicamente y no se tomaron medidas efectivas, supuestamente porque el propietario es autista.

El autismo no es una luz verde para maltratar a los animales. Un diagnóstico puede cambiar CÓMO intervienen las autoridades (enfoque seguro, apoyo presente). No cambia SI las autoridades intervienen. Si una persona no puede tener un animal sin recurrir a la violencia, el animal debe ser retirado.

Medidas necesarias:
1) Comprobación inmediata del bienestar y evaluación de riesgos en el lugar en cuestión.
2) Retirada protectora/salvaguarda del perro.
3) Evaluación veterinaria y documentación.
4) Investigación formal y medidas coercitivas adecuadas.

No se dejaría a un niño en un hogar abusivo simplemente porque uno de los padres tiene una discapacidad. El principio de protección es el mismo: proteger primero a la víctima vulnerable.

Atentamente,

Equipo de @HallieForAnimals
contact@hallieforanimals.org


To whom it may concern,

This concerns the recent animal abuse case in Engativa, Bogota (Altos de Granada residential complex) where a dog was reportedly physically assaulted and no effective action was taken, allegedly because the owner is autistic.

Autism is not a green light to abuse animals. A diagnosis can change HOW authorities intervene (safe approach, support present). It does not change WHETHER authorities intervene. If a person cannot keep an animal without violence, the animal must be removed.

Required actions:
1) Immediate welfare check and risk assessment at the location in question
2) Protective removal/safeguarding of the dog
3) Veterinary assessment and documentation
4) Formal investigation and appropriate enforcement action

You would not leave a child in an abusive household simply because a parent has a disability. The safeguarding principle is the same: protect the vulnerable victim first.

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Pass L35/2026 End Mass Killing in Romania
links.descriptionLong:
Romanian committees will meet to discuss the methods of controlling street dog populations next week.
The aim is to push for a more humane TNVR approach.
links.mailtoSubject: Pasul L35/2026 Sfârșitul uciderii în masă/Pass L35/2026 End Mass Killing
links.mailtoBody:
Către cine de drept,

Vă scriu în legătură cu L35/2026 (care modifică OUG 155/2001) privind gestionarea câinilor fără stăpân.

România are de ales între două opțiuni clare: o politică modernă și eficientă, axată pe prevenire, sau repetarea aceluiași ciclu eșuat de capturare și ucidere, care nu rezolvă cauza problemei.

NE AȘTEPTĂM CA DUMNEAVOASTRĂ SĂ:

1) SĂ SPRIJINIȚI L35/2026 CU UN RAPORT FAVORABIL ȘI SĂ ÎL MENȚINEȚI PUTERNIC.
Eutanasia în masă a câinilor sănătoși nu este „gestionare”. Este eliminare. Nu oprește reproducerea necontrolată sau abandonul. Uciderea este un cerc vicios. Prevenirea este soluția.

2) SĂ PROTJAȚI SIGURANȚA PUBLICĂ ÎN MOD CORECT (NU ÎN MOD UȘOR).
Dacă preocuparea este reprezentată de mușcături, haite și riscul public, atunci legiferați ceea ce reduce efectiv riscul:
- sterilizarea pe scară largă
- microciparea și înregistrarea
- sancțiuni reale pentru abandon și pentru proprietarii care permit reproducerea necontrolată
- programe finanțate de capturare-sterilizare-returnare/replasare cu raportare transparentă
- standarde, audituri și trasabilitate pentru adăposturi, astfel încât „câinii dispăruți” și corupția să nu se poată ascunde în spatele documentelor

3) MENȚINEȚI EUTANASIA DOAR CA O EXCEPȚIE MEDICALĂ ÎNTR-O SITUAȚIE EXCEPȚIONALĂ.
Eutanasia trebuie limitată la cazurile de boli incurabile, suferințe severe sau câini periculoși confirmați, pentru care reabilitarea nu este posibilă. Nu trebuie să fie soluția implicită pentru animalele sănătoase după o perioadă scurtă de detenție.

DE CE ESTE IMPORTANT:
- Uciderea câinilor sănătoși este indefensibilă din punct de vedere etic și normalizează cruzimea ca „politică”.
- De asemenea, agravează siguranța publică pe termen lung, evitând prevenirea și responsabilitatea.
- Publicul urmărește cum veți vota și ce veți recomanda săptămâna viitoare.

Ne așteptăm la un raport favorabil și la nicio modificare care să slăbească scopul principal al L35/2026: încetarea uciderii în masă și înlocuirea acesteia cu o gestionare care pune prevenirea pe primul plan.

Cu sinceritate,

Echipa @HallieForAnimals
contact@hallieforanimals.org



To whom it may concern,

I am writing regarding L35/2026 (amending OUG 155/2001) on the management of dogs without an owner.

Romania has a clear choice: modern, effective prevention-first policy, or repeating the same failed cycle of capture-and-kill that does not solve the source of the problem.

WE EXPECT YOU TO:

1) SUPPORT L35/2026 WITH A FAVORABLE REPORT AND KEEP IT STRONG.
Mass euthanasia of healthy dogs is not "management." It is disposal. It does not stop uncontrolled breeding or abandonment. Killing is a treadmill. Prevention is a solution.

2) PROTECT PUBLIC SAFETY THE RIGHT WAY (NOT THE EASY WAY).
If the concern is bites, packs, and public risk, then legislate what actually reduces risk:
- large-scale sterilisation
- microchipping and registration
- real penalties for abandonment and for owners who allow uncontrolled breeding
- funded catch-sterilise-return / rehome programs with transparent reporting
- shelter standards, audits, and traceability so "disappearing dogs" and corruption cannot hide behind paperwork

3) KEEP EUTHANASIA AS A NARROW MEDICAL EXCEPTION ONLY.
Euthanasia must be limited to cases of incurable illness, severe suffering, or confirmed dangerous dogs where rehabilitation is not possible. It must not be the default outcome for healthy animals after a short holding period.

WHY THIS MATTERS:
- Killing healthy dogs is ethically indefensible and normalises cruelty as "policy."
- It also makes public safety worse long-term by avoiding prevention and accountability.
- The public is watching how you vote and what you recommend next week.

We expect a favorable report and no amendments that weaken the core purpose of L35/2026: ending mass killing and replacing it with prevention-first management.

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

To whom it may concern,

This message concerns CF-2026-00018 (State of Oklahoma vs. Lauren Renee Wright), filed as CRUELTY TO ANIMALS (B5).

The community is demanding the full extent of the law be applied. No charge reduction. No quiet deal that minimizes what happened. No outcome that amounts to a wrist-slap.

REQUESTS:

1. Oppose any plea reduction or dismissal that minimizes accountability in CF-2026-00018.
2. Oppose any deferred-style resolution that avoids a meaningful conviction-level outcome for felony animal cruelty.
3. Seek the strongest lawful sentence available, including custody where authorized, restitution, and strict supervision terms.
4. Maintain and enforce the "NO POSSESSION OF ANIMALS" condition, and pursue a long-term/lifetime prohibition on owning, possessing, or residing with animals as part of sentencing conditions.
5. Ensure the case file is complete and prosecution-ready: preserve and present all veterinary documentation, photos, witness statements, and all agency reports. If other animals were involved or at risk, investigate fully and file additional counts where supported by evidence.

This request is strengthened by the defendant's documented prior criminal case in Woodward County:

* CM-2025-00042, DUI-ALCOHOL-AGGRAVATED (MISDEMEANOR), filed 02/25/2025.
  The docket reflects a plea setting completed 06/24/2025 and a deferred-style status ("ST PENDING DEFERRED") on record.

When a person is already appearing in court for a serious alcohol-related driving offense, the correct response to a felony cruelty case is not another soft landing. The public expects consistent accountability and a clear deterrent message.

Sincerely,

links.mailtoSubject: Request For Investigation: Dog Death & Cover Up
links.mailtoBody:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to submit a formal complaint and request immediate enforcement action regarding a Kansas City pet grooming business operating as "Grisel's Puppies"/"Grisel's Puppies LLC."

1. BUSINESS IDENTIFIERS (PRIMARY SOURCE: MISSOURI SOS ENTITY RECORD)

Legal entity name: GRISEL'S PUPPIES LLC
Entity type: Domestic Limited Liability Company
Status: Active
Charter no.: LC014329374
Date formed: 10/18/2021
Duration: Perpetual

Principal office address:
539 E 27th Ave Apt 1C
Kansas City, MO 64116-3062

Registered agent:
OLAZAR DE MELGAREJO, GRISELDA B

Registered agent address:
539 E 27th Ave Apt 1C
Kansas City, MO 64116-3062

2. OPERATING LOCATION (PUBLIC BUSINESS LISTINGS)

Operating / customer-facing address publicly listed:
3805 N Oak Trfy
Kansas City, MO 64116

3. INCIDENT SUMMARY (TEDDY)

Incident involves a dog ("Teddy," approx. 3 years old) dropped off for a routine bath/brush.

Timeline (as reported by the owner, consistent with supporting materials):

Teddy was dropped off for grooming.
Approximately 1 hour after drop-off, the groomer indicated Teddy was "ready."
Upon the owner's arrival for pickup, staff stated Teddy had "escaped" approximately 10 minutes earlier.
The family and multiple volunteers searched all night into the morning.
Teddy was found deceased ("cold body") approximately 2 miles away, in the opposite direction staff allegedly indicated Teddy went.

Material concerns:

The escape explanation provided by staff is highly questionable and appears inconsistent with basic containment and supervision.
When the owner requested relevant camera footage (including cameras "over the door"), they were told the camera batteries were dead.
The business allegedly did not assist meaningfully with search efforts and did not post the dog as missing on their page.

4. LAWYER CONTACT / OFFERS (DOCUMENTED)

Screenshots available show written offers from counsel, including:

A) Offer of $1,500.00 plus full cremation reimbursement described as "sympathy/goodwill."

B) Subsequent offer the next day explicitly stating the lawyer "did a little research into the cost of labradoodle puppies" and that the client(s) were willing to offer $2,000.00 and reimburse cremation upon receipt.

These offers were presented in exchange for silence/resolution. The complainant declined and began posting about the incident.

5. IDENTITY / RESPONSIBILITY INCONSISTENCY (RELEVANT FOR ENFORCEMENT)

The Missouri SOS record ties the LLC and registered agent to:
OLAZAR DE MELGAREJO, GRISELDA B

However, a lawyer letter provided to the family states the attorney represents:
Sergio Ruiz, described as the owner/operator of the grooming business that cared for Teddy.

We request that your office determine:

Which individual(s) had custody and control of the animal at the time of loss,
Which legal entity accepted payment and provided services,
Whether any DBAs or additional entities are involved,
Who is responsible for operational safety procedures, staffing, and records retention.

6. REQUESTED ACTIONS

1) OPEN A FORMAL INVESTIGATION
   - Open an official case and assign an investigator. Provide a case/reference number in writing.

2) IMMEDIATE EVIDENCE PRESERVATION / SPOLIATION WARNING
   - Issue a written preservation directive to the business and any associated individuals/entities requiring retention of ALL records related to Teddy, including:
     a) scheduling logs, intake notes, kennel assignment, incident reports
     b) staff roster, staff statements, training/procedure documents
     c) internal messages (texts/apps/emails) concerning the incident
     d) CCTV/video files (interior/exterior/door area), door/entry logs if any
     e) camera maintenance records, charging/power method, storage/retention policy
   - If the business claims footage is unavailable (e.g., "dead batteries"), require a sworn written explanation identifying:
     a) which cameras existed, exact coverage areas, and their power/storage setup
     b) who was responsible for ensuring operability, and the last confirmed working date/time
     c) what steps were taken to retrieve or preserve any existing footage
   - Treat loss/destruction of relevant evidence after notice as potential spoliation/obstruction, and refer as appropriate.

3) PREMISES INSPECTION (OPERATING LOCATION)
   - Inspect the operating premises for containment and supervision controls:
     kennels, door latches/closures, fencing, handoff procedures, supervision ratios, and any documented protocols for escape prevention and emergency response.

4) LICENSING / REGISTRATION / COMPLIANCE REVIEW
   - Verify the business is properly licensed/registered for the services offered at the operating location.
   - Confirm whether additional permits apply (e.g., daycare/boarding if offered), and take corrective enforcement action for any noncompliance.

5) CRIMINAL / ENFORCEMENT REFERRAL REVIEW
   - Evaluate whether conduct in this matter meets thresholds for criminal investigation and/or referral to the appropriate prosecutorial authority, including negligence or other offenses applicable to an animal death in commercial custody, and any potential obstruction-related concerns tied to missing footage or misleading statements.

6) CONSUMER PROTECTION / UNFAIR PRACTICES REVIEW
   - Review the documented settlement offers routed through counsel (including statements referencing "research into the cost of labradoodle puppies" and offers tied to resolution/silence) alongside allegations of deleting/blocking reports.
   - Determine whether this constitutes unfair/deceptive practices or conduct relevant to licensing fitness, and pursue administrative action where available.

7) PATTERN ANALYSIS / COMPLAINT CORRELATION
   - Determine whether additional complaints exist (injury, escape, death, unsafe handling).
   - If a pattern is identified, escalate response accordingly (enhanced inspection frequency, license action, referral, or closure procedures where authorized).

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1wiGBNm1nmnSDP4uIrV3UWPbP-3spJbeR?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

إلى من يهمه الأمر،

هذا تقرير رسمي عن حالة قسوة شديدة على الحيوانات وتهديد للسلامة العامة.

تعرض كلب للضرب المبرح (بالهراوة) في مكان عام. لم يكن هذا حادثًا عرضيًا أو ”تأديبًا“. بل كان قسوة متعمدة ومستمرة تمت علنًا أمام شهود.

القاتل المبلغ عنه: فؤاد سليم
الموقع المبلغ عنه: قرية خارسيت، شارع الشيخ محي المزي، المعادي، مصر.

هذا المستوى من القسوة ليس ”مجرد مشكلة تتعلق بالحيوانات“. أعمال العنف العلنية هي علامة تحذيرية على تصعيد العنف. الشخص الذي يضرب حيوانًا أعزل في الشارع يظهر قدرته على إيذاء الناس أيضًا. تعاملوا مع هذا الأمر على أنه عنف، وليس نزاعًا.

الإجراءات الفورية المطلوبة:

1. تعامل مع المشتبه به على أنه خطر عام. حدد هويته على الفور واتخذ إجراءات إنفاذية دون تأخير. يجب السعي إلى فرض عقوبة الحبس. الغرامة الرمزية ليست نتيجة ولا تمنع تكرار الجريمة.
2. قم بتأمين وحفظ جميع الأدلة الآن، قبل أن يتم حذفها أو استبدالها:
   * الفيديو (الفيديوهات) الأصلي (الأصلية)، والتحميلات، والحساب (الحسابات) الأول (الأولى) الذي نشرها.
   * كاميرات المراقبة من المتاجر/المنازل القريبة وأي كاميرات مواجهة للشارع (عاجل: العديد من الأنظمة تقوم بالكتابة فوقها بسرعة).
   * إفادات الشهود من السكان (يتم أخذها على الفور، كتابةً).
   * أي تفاصيل تعريفية: الوجه، الصوت، الملابس، علامات الموقع الدقيقة، المركبات، اللوحات، المرافقين.

3. اذهب إلى الموقع المحدد على الفور لتحديد مكان الحيوان وتوثيق المشهد:
   * إذا كان الحيوان على قيد الحياة: قدم له الرعاية البيطرية العاجلة واحتجزه لحمايته، مع توثيق كامل للإصابات طبياً.
   * إذا كان الحيوان ميتاً: تعامل مع الموقع على أنه مسرح جريمة، وقم بتصويره بالفيديو أو الفوتوغرافيا، واحتفظ بأي أدلة مادية متعلقة بالحادث، وقم بتوثيق الرفات من خلال القنوات الرسمية.
4. فتح تحقيق جنائي وتطبيق أقسى العقوبات القانونية المتاحة. ضرب حيوان في الأماكن العامة هو قسوة متعمدة. هذه ليست مشكلة خلافية ولا مسألة تستدعي التحذير.
5. عدم السماح بإغلاق القضية بغرامة تافهة. الغرامة التي يمكن العثور عليها في وسائد الأريكة ليست رادعًا. إنها إذن. إذا لم تتم مقاضاة هذا المشتبه به بشكل صحيح، فمن المرجح أن يكرر فعلته، ومن المحتمل أن يتصاعد العنف ضد الأشخاص.
6. إذا كان أي مسؤول محلي قد رفض أو قلل من أهمية هذا البلاغ في السابق، فقم بالتحقيق في هذا الإخفاق. إن تجاهل القسوة العامة يشجع على المزيد من العنف ويقوض سلامة المجتمع.

المساءلة:

* يجب ألا يتم تجاهل هذه القضية أو التقليل من أهميتها أو التعامل معها بشكل غير رسمي.
* الأدلة تختفي بسرعة. كاميرات المراقبة تسجل فوقها. الشهود يتعرضون للضغط. تصرف على الفور.

الأدلة: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gL7BvInCPpREARt5MTNbhPVmOzRFaBXE/view?usp=sharing

مع خالص الشكر والتقدير،

فريق @HallieForAnimals
contact@hallieforanimals.org


To whom it may concern,

This is a formal report of extreme animal cruelty and a public safety threat.

A dog was violently beaten (bludgeoned) in public. This was not an accident or “discipline.” It is deliberate, sustained cruelty carried out openly, in front of witnesses.

Reported killer: Fouad Selim
Reported location: Kharseet village, Sheikh Mohie El-Mouzi Street, Maadi, Egypt.

This level of cruelty is not “just an animal issue.” Public acts of violence are a warning sign for escalation. A person who will beat a helpless animal in the street is demonstrating the capacity to harm people as well. Treat this as violence, not a dispute.

IMMEDIATE ACTIONS REQUIRED:

1. Treat the suspect as a public danger. Identify him immediately and take enforcement action without delay. Custodial punishment must be pursued. A token fine is not an outcome and will not prevent reoffending.
2. Secure and preserve all evidence NOW, before it is deleted or overwritten:

   * The original video(s), uploads, and the first account(s) that posted them
   * CCTV from nearby shops/homes and any street-facing cameras (urgent: many systems overwrite quickly)
   * Witness statements from residents (taken promptly, in writing)
   * Any identifying details: face, voice, clothing, exact location markers, vehicles, plates, companions
3. Attend the exact location immediately to locate the animal and document the scene:
   * If alive: urgent veterinary care and seizure for protection, with full medical documentation of injuries
   * If deceased: treat as a crime scene, photograph/video the scene, preserve any physical evidence involved, and document the remains through official channels
4. Open a criminal investigation and apply the strongest available legal penalties. Beating an animal in public is deliberate cruelty. This is not a dispute and not a matter for warnings.
5. Do not allow this to be closed with a trivial fine. A fine that can be found in couch cushions is not deterrence. It is permission. If this suspect is not prosecuted properly, he is likely to do it again, and escalation to violence against people is a credible risk.
6. If any local responder previously dismissed or minimized this report, investigate that failure. Ignoring public cruelty encourages further violence and undermines community safety.

ACCOUNTABILITY:

* This case must not be ignored, minimized, or handled informally.
* Evidence disappears quickly. CCTV overwrites. Witnesses get pressured. Act immediately.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gL7BvInCPpREARt5MTNbhPVmOzRFaBXE/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

To whom it may concern,

This is a formal report of extreme animal cruelty and a public safety threat.

A dog was hanged in the street: a rope was pulled/raised so the dog was left suspended by the neck. This was not an accident. It is deliberate torture carried out in public, in front of witnesses.

Reported location: Kharseet village, Sheikh Mohie El-Mouzi Street, Tanta, Egypt.

This level of cruelty is not "just an animal issue." Public acts of violence are a warning sign for escalation. A person who will hang a living animal in the street is demonstrating the capacity to harm people as well. Treat this as violence, not a dispute.

IMMEDIATE ACTIONS REQUIRED:
1) Treat this suspect as a public danger. Identify him immediately and take enforcement action without delay. Prison time must be pursued. A token fine is not an outcome and will not prevent reoffending.
2) Secure and preserve all evidence NOW, before it is deleted or overwritten:
   - The original video(s), uploads, and the first account(s) that posted them
   - CCTV from nearby shops/homes and any street-facing cameras (urgent: many systems overwrite quickly)
   - Witness statements from residents (taken promptly, in writing)
   - Any identifying details: face, voice, clothing, exact location markers, vehicles, plates, companions
3) Attend the exact location immediately to locate the animal and document the scene:
   - If alive: urgent veterinary care and seizure for protection, with full medical documentation
   - If deceased: treat as a crime scene, photograph/video the scene, preserve the rope/ligature and any other physical evidence, and document the remains through official channels
4) Open a criminal investigation and apply the strongest available legal penalties, specifically seeking custodial punishment. Hanging an animal by the neck in public is deliberate torture. This is not a dispute and not a matter for warnings.
5) Do not allow this to be closed with a trivial fine. A fine that can be found in couch cushions is not deterrence. It is permission. If this suspect is not imprisoned, he is likely to do it again, and escalation to violence against people is a credible risk.
6) If any local responder previously dismissed or minimized this report, investigate that failure. Ignoring public torture encourages further violence and undermines community safety.

ACCOUNTABILITY:
- This case must not be ignored, minimized, or handled informally.
- Evidence disappears quickly. CCTV overwrites. Witnesses get pressured. Act immediately.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1r-R8fYpCnwSGkqpV_LCfRRre8ZlCWKxX/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.mailtoSubject: Emaciated Dog at  1425 Sterling Pl, Brooklyn
links.mailtoBody:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am reporting an ongoing animal welfare concern at:

1425 Sterling Place
Brooklyn, NY

Today, NYPD responded and left without meaningful assessment, stating only that “the dog looks fine,” apologizing to the homeowner, and clearing the call.

Sending a standard patrol officer with zero animal-welfare training to assess neglect/exposure is like sending an optician to fill in for a gynecologist. These calls require responders trained to evaluate neglect, malnutrition, exposure risk, shelter adequacy, restraint/tethering compliance, and health indicators that are not obvious to a casual glance.

The dog appears EMACIATED. Even from a distance, ribs are visibly protruding. This is not a subjective “I don’t like how the dog looks” complaint, visible ribs at a distance indicates a potentially dangerous body condition requiring immediate evaluation.

This report involves potential neglect/malnutrition and may also involve outdoor exposure and/or unlawful restraint. In New York City and New York State, “looks fine” is not a legal or professional standard.

Relevant legal standards include:

* NY Agriculture & Markets Law § 353: cruelty/neglect, including failure to provide proper sustenance.
* NY Agriculture & Markets Law § 353-b: dogs left outdoors must be provided appropriate shelter suitable to the climate and the dog’s condition.

Requested actions (time-critical):

1. Immediate follow-up welfare check by a supervisor-qualified unit and/or an animal-welfare-trained responder, with full documented assessment of:

   * body condition score / visible signs of malnutrition,
   * access to adequate food and unfrozen water,
   * veterinary evaluation if the dog appears underweight, ill, weak, or dehydrated,
   * shelter adequacy (weatherproof, insulated, dry, size-appropriate)

2. Immediate protective action as permitted under law, including seizure/impound for veterinary care if necessary.

3. Internal review of the initial responding officer’s handling:

   * why the call was dismissed despite visible indicators of poor condition,
   * what objective checks were performed (photos, notes, questions asked, water/food verification),
   * whether a supervisor was consulted on a welfare complaint involving suspected malnutrition.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Qcb9qOr-035U1NY2PuH8lqoVSYVSanDX/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
@contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.mailtoSubject: Miniature Schnauzer Kicked During Arrest Operation
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am submitting a formal misconduct complaint regarding a viral video showing a U.S. Marshal in Memphis (Whitehaven area) turning and kicking a Miniature Schanuzer during an arrest operation.

The dog, named "Yoshi", was later diagnosed by a veterinarian with a broken rib. The incident occurred during a USMS-led arrest operation outside a Whitehaven apartment complex in Memphis, Tennessee.

This complaint concerns BOTH:
1) the individual deputy's conduct (use of force against a small domestic animal), and
2) the operational decision-making and supervision during the arrest operation (risk assessment, de-escalation, and handling of non-threats in a residential environment).

A federal officer is trained to assess threat in the moment. A miniature schnauzer is not a lethal threat. If the official position is "officer safety," then produce the records and footage that supposedly justify that claim.

Actions demanded:

1) Open a misconduct investigation into the deputy who kicked the dog.
2) Conduct an operational/force review of the entire arrest operation (supervision, decision-making, escalation).
3) Identify the USMS district/task force unit involved and the chain of command responsible for the operation.
4) Preserve all evidence immediately, including:
   - all body-worn camera footage (full, unedited),
   - any assisting-agency dash/body camera footage,
   - incident reports, use-of-force documentation, and any after-action materials,
   - any veterinary records or evidentiary materials obtained by the agency.
5) Release the full, unedited footage to the maximum extent permitted. If any portion is withheld, publish the legal basis and scope of the withholding.

This is a conduct and accountability problem. If the agency believes the kick was justified, that determination must be supported by the full, unedited footage and documentation, not a one-paragraph statement.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ZK8np9IZb5WyJlMSktCPTm1Cy4HykafH/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

To whom it may concern,

I am reporting ongoing animal cruelty at:

2826 Willow Pl, South Gate, CA 90280

The dog at this address is being beaten regularly (reported as daily). Police response to date has been ineffective and the abuse is continuing. SEAACA also indicated they would help, but the dog remains in danger.

Suspect's vehicle: silver/gray 2007 Ford D-150, California plate 8L85950.

Under California law, officers/animal control do not need to wait for visible injuries before removing an animal when it is at risk. California Penal Code 597.1 authorizes immediate seizure/impoundment when an officer has reasonable grounds to believe that very prompt action is required to protect the health or safety of the animal (or others). Given repeated reports of daily beating at this address, the threshold for prompt action is met and the dog must be removed for safety pursuant to Penal Code 597.1, and investigated as cruelty under Penal Code 597.

ACTION DEMANDED:

1. Immediate welfare check with an Animal Control Officer present, and seizure/impound of the dog for veterinary assessment and safety pursuant to Penal Code 597.1.
2. Criminal investigation for animal cruelty under Penal Code 597, including evidence collection, witness canvass, and body-worn camera documentation.
3. Supervisor review of prior calls for service at this address and written documentation of why prior responses did not result in protection of the animal.
4. If entry is refused, escalate appropriately for lawful access consistent with officer safety and agency procedures.
5. Public statement of the agency findings and proceedings taken in response to this report (to the extent permitted by law), including confirmation that the dog is safe and the case has been formally handled.

This is time-critical. Animal cruelty is a public safety issue and frequently escalates. This report should be cross-referenced against prior calls for service and any prior SEAACA interactions tied to this address and/or vehicle.

links.mailtoSubject: Justice For Hit & Run Dogs In Manaus
links.mailtoBody:
Assunto: Incidente envolvendo a PMAM hoje em Manaus — dois cães mortos; exigência de suspensão, demissão e acusações criminais

A quem possa interessar,

Estou enviando uma reclamação formal sobre um incidente ocorrido na manhã de 1º de fevereiro em Manaus, Amazonas, no qual um veículo identificado como unidade da Polícia Militar do Amazonas (PMAM) é flagrado por câmeras de segurança atropelando e matando dois cães e continuando sem parar.

Esta é uma exigência de responsabilização adequada. Se a PMAM e o Estado do Amazonas desejam ter alguma credibilidade, os policiais envolvidos devem ser suspensos imediatamente e, assim que a investigação confirmar o que as imagens já mostram, devem ser demitidos e acusados criminalmente.

O que estamos exigindo

1. Suspensão imediata dos policiais envolvidos, a partir de agora, durante o período da investigação.
   “Funções administrativas” não são responsabilização.

2. Demissão (rescisão) assim que a investigação confirmar os fatos mostrados no vídeo:
   Atropelar dois animais e continuar sem parar é incompatível com o serviço público e o policiamento.

The City is now playing the victim and refusing to let Gilda be adopted out of "an abundance of precaution".
The City of Dallas doesn't get to play the victim and take revenge on a dog for responding predictably to capture pressure.
links.mailtoSubject: Immediate Hold Request - Gilda - Bite Protocol, Due Process, Transfer Plan
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing regarding Dallas Animal Services case A1276308 (Gilda). This message is a direct request that Dallas Animal Services not euthanize this dog and instead pursue reasonable, controlled alternatives.

An email response has circulated attributed to Mr. Ramon, sent to another concerned party, stating:
"As of now, based on the injuries to the victim, this animal will not be allowed to be released out of an abundance of precaution."

I am referencing this because it appears to reflect the current position for A1276308 and frames the decision as precaution-based.

REQUEST:
Do not euthanize A1276308. Give her a chance through controlled alternatives.

Gilda was located and captured by a good samaritan. A bite that occurs in the context of a high-stress capture of a fearful, escaped dog is a foreseeable risk. That is not automatic proof that the dog is inherently dangerous. It is often the predictable result of fear, panic, and handling under pressure.

Mr. Ramon's phrase "out of an abundance of precaution" is backwards. Precaution is what you do BEFORE someone gets hurt, not a slogan used afterward to justify an irreversible outcome. If precaution were truly the priority, the focus would be on prevention and safe handling:

* enforce outdoor restraint and extreme weather standards before a dog is pushed to escape
* prioritize safe, low-stress capture procedures and equipment
* treat this as a prevention and neglect failure first, not a "bite case" to dispose of

If "abundance of precaution" is the standard, then explain what preventive precautions were taken before this incident, and why euthanasia is being treated as the first and only "precautionary" option now.

A decision to kill A1276308 based on a bite arising from a high-stress capture context will look like the City punishing an animal for responding predictably to conditions humans created. That outcome is not a genuine safety measure and it is not a defensible use of the word "precaution." It's nothing short of revenge.

If public safety is the concern, the safer and more defensible approach is controlled safeguards and evaluation, not summary euthanasia:

* comply with required bite protocol/observation as applicable
* secure confinement with strict handling controls (muzzle protocol where appropriate)
* behavior evaluation by a qualified professional
* open a clear placement pathway with a qualified rescue or experienced handler under documented containment conditions

WE ARE REQUESTING TWO SPECIFIC ACTIONS:

1. PUBLIC STATEMENT
   Issue a public statement clarifying the City's position on A1276308. At minimum, the statement should confirm:

* whether A1276308 is still scheduled for euthanasia on February 4
* whether the City will consider a live outcome for A1276308

2. OPEN A1276308 TO QUALIFIED RESCUE
   Open A1276308 to qualified rescue/experienced placement and publish:

* the email/point of contact to submit rescue offers
* the minimum requirements to be considered (secure containment plan, handling protocols, bite-protocol compliance, etc.)

We are asking the City to choose the option that is both safer and more defensible: controlled safeguards and evaluation, followed by placement if appropriate. The City should not kill a dog whose behavior may have been an entirely predictable result of neglect, fear, and capture pressure.

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Stop Chinese Cat Torture Rings
links.descriptionLong:
Our kids are being exposed to extreme animal torture content, cats and kittens abused for entertainment, status, and in some cases, paid commissioning.
Reports claim 43% of 16–17 year olds have witnessed animal cruelty online. A generation is being conditioned to tolerate suffering.

These cruelty rings don’t survive because the internet is “too big to police.”
They survive because the source is allowed to operate, and the content is then exported globally through reuploads and private channels.
As of today, there are zero animal welfare laws in China. In 2006, a comprehensive animal welfare bill was drafted, but the Chinese government refused to move it forward.

Animal cruelty is a known red flag for human violence.
Research into school massacres found 43% of perpetrators had histories of animal cruelty before the attacks.
A study of 150 adult males arrested for animal cruelty/neglect/animal sexual abuse found 96% had other criminal charges in their histories.

Torture content → desensitisation → escalation → harm to people

China must shut these networks down at the source.
Not “investigate.” Not “monitor.” Not “condemn.”
Arrest, prosecute, and dismantle.

And if China refuses to police torture-for-entertainment networks operating inside its borders, then governments across the world need to stop treating this like an awkward cultural footnote and start treating it like what it is:

A cross-border youth harm crisis.

That’s why we are contacting the international embassies in China:

Because this isn’t staying in China.
It’s landing in our kids’ feeds.
links.mailtoSubject: China must shut down torture-to-order networks at the source
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

We are demanding urgent diplomatic and law-enforcement pressure on China to shut down organised online networks producing and distributing extreme animal cruelty content, including torture-to-order content targeting cats and kittens.

This is a youth safety issue, a violence normalisation issue, and a failure to police supply at the source.

Multiple investigations and watchdog reporting have described extreme cat torture networks as being produced and coordinated in China, then distributed globally through private channels, reuploads, and paid commissioning. When the source jurisdiction fails to enforce against producers and organisers, the rest of the world is left chasing mirrors and middlemen.

This problem is not solvable by chasing uploads alone. It requires enforcement where the content is being made and organised.

Children are already being exposed at scale.
The RSPCA Animal Kindness Index reports that 43% of 16-17 year olds have witnessed animal cruelty online. This is mainstream exposure to violence.

Animal cruelty is a recognised warning sign for violence toward humans.
This is not theory. It shows up repeatedly in research and law enforcement analysis, including links to domestic violence, child abuse, and serious violent offending.

71% of women entering domestic violence shelters reported that an abusive partner had threatened, injured, or killed their pets.
Animal abuse occurs in 88% of homes where child physical abuse is present.
Around 43% of school shooters (1988-2012) had histories of animal cruelty.

Animal cruelty rarely exists in isolation.
In one study of 150 offenders charged in cases involving animal cruelty and related offenses, 96% had other criminal charges in their histories. In plain terms: animal abusers are frequently repeat offenders, and cruelty is often part of a wider pattern.

Extreme abuse material clusters.
In cases involving CSAM (child sexual abuse material), it is common to see additional abuse material involving animals. This reflects a broader, documented pattern of escalating and overlapping harmful content consumption. This is exactly why animal torture networks must be treated as safeguarding and public safety issues, not "content moderation problems."

This is the pipeline we are warning about:
torture content -> desensitisation -> escalation -> harm to people.

If China does not act decisively against the production and commissioning of this content, it is allowing violent desensitisation to be exported into childrens feeds worldwide.

We are calling for immediate action and public accountability from China to:

1. Identify, arrest, and prosecute the organisers, producers, and commissioners
This must include:

* torture-to-order organisers
* content producers
* admins/moderators
* payment handlers
* distributors and reupload coordinators

2. Prioritise these crimes as serious offences
Torture-for-entertainment networks are not a minor nuisance. They are exporting extreme violence globally and exposing minors to it at scale.

3. Cooperate with international enforcement
These networks are cross-border by design. China must cooperate with international cybercrime units to disrupt the wider ecosystem: distribution hubs, purchasers, and funding routes.

4. Provide public accountability and measurable results
We are not asking for private replies. We are demanding public action:

* a public statement acknowledging the existence of these networks
* a clear enforcement plan led by Chinese authorities
* regular published updates on arrests and disruption outcomes

We are asking your government to raise this directly with Chinese authorities as a child safety and public safety issue, not merely an "animal welfare" concern.

A jurisdiction that allows torture-content producers to operate with weak deterrence becomes the supply hub for global desensitisation and escalating violence.

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.mailtoSubject: Corona Animal Control Officers Mishandling
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing regarding an incident that occurred on January 15th 2026 at a Corona, CA apartment complex where Corona Animal Services officers responded to a call about a loose dog.

Residents contacted Animal Control in good faith, hoping the dog would be safely secured and reunited with its owner. The dog was described by multiple residents and Corona shelter staff as friendly, calm, and social, he approached people safely and was even playing with children.

Instead of using calm, low-stress handling appropriate for a non-aggressive dog, three officers arrived with catch poles and forcibly wrestled the dog into the back of their vehicle. This was excessive, unnecessary, and showed a complete lack of care for the animal’s welfare.

Catch poles may be necessary in certain situations for officer safety, but using them aggressively on a friendly, non-threatening dog is reckless. It creates panic, causes injuries, and escalates fear-based reactions. This is the exact kind of handling that can cause a dog to bite in the first place.

This incident requires immediate accountability and corrective action.

We are demanding the City of Corona take the following actions:

1. Immediate internal review and investigation of this incident, including officer conduct and compliance with humane handling standards.
2. Identification of the officers involved and confirmation that appropriate disciplinary action will be taken.
3. Mandatory training and clear protocol updates on low-stress dog handling and capture methods, including when catch poles are appropriate and when they are not.
4. A public statement from Corona Animal Services/the City confirming what actions will be taken to prevent this from happening again.

Animal Control exists to protect animals and the community. What happened here was avoidable, and the public should not be afraid to call for help because they fear a calm dog will be handled like a violent threat.

The public is watching closely. This must be taken seriously.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xZRAsMpO2Xje1deEe-vHKhGML9M4RvVH/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

This is a request for immediate investigation into suspected animal cruelty and unlawful/inhumane handling of wildlife.

IDENTIFIERS

* Name: Henry Lee Wofford
* Address: 908 Ida Street, Wildwood, FL 34785
* Business name used publicly: COON BOYZ LLC

ALLEGATION (as depicted)
A raccoon was trapped, rendered unconscious or incapacitated, and then nailed to a sheet of plywood.

Before anyone tries the predictable excuse, please note:
Post-mortem movements can occur, including reflex twitching and brief agonal gasps that can resemble breathing. That reality does NOT excuse what is shown. If the animal was not definitively confirmed dead before being nailed, this may have been prolonged suffering. If the animal was already dead, it is still grotesque mutilation and improper handling/disposal. Either way, it warrants investigation and enforcement action.

REQUESTED ACTIONS

1. Open an investigation and confirm the lead agency (Wildwood PD / Sumter County Sheriff) and whether FWC is engaged for wildlife law violations.
2. Conduct an immediate welfare check of the property for additional trapped/injured animals and any inhumane trapping practices.
3. Secure and preserve evidence: traps, tools/fasteners, boards used, any remains, and any footage. If remains exist, ensure proper collection/documentation and, if applicable, veterinary/forensic examination.
4. Determine whether the person/company is operating as a nuisance-wildlife service and whether methods comply with Florida law and humane standards.
5. Issue a PUBLIC STATEMENT confirming this incident is being investigated and advising the public how to submit tips/evidence.

If this does not meet the threshold for immediate action, then the threshold is the problem.

Animal cruelty is strongly associated with broader patterns of violence and escalation. Soft outcomes in cases this brazen do not protect the public, they train offenders that the system will look away.

links.title: Prosecute Kristi Hicks

Multiple German Shepherds and Belgian Malinois were found on her property in severe neglect, many were dead, including dead puppies in the freezer; some reports claim over 120 dogs were found.
links.mailtoSubject: Hopkins County: Zero Tolerance for Animal Cruelty
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing to demand that Hopkins County treats the Hicks animal cruelty case with the seriousness it warrants and pursues accountability to the fullest extent of the law.

If Hopkins County wants the public to believe it has any credible deterrent against animal abuse, this is the moment to prove it.

Reported facts are straightforward and sickening:

* A dog was tied to a tree.
* The dog was shot at point blank range three times.
* Approximately three minutes passed between the first and third shot, meaning the dog suffered while the shooter reloaded with the tact of a quadruple amputee.

This was an execution. 

Reports indicate the same situation involved other dogs living in neglectful conditions, including reports of deceased dogs on or near the property, some reports claiming over 120 dogs on the premisis. Neglect isn't a separate, softer category of violence, it's prolonged suffering, and is often the same pipeline that ends in overt cruelty. If there were other animals involved, every animal must be accounted for, every condition documented, and every offense charged where supported by evidence.

What I am requesting (non-negotiable):

1. Treat this as a serious cruelty case, not a minor incident.
*Preserve all evidence immediately (video, witness statements, 911 calls, bodycam, animal control notes, firearm evidence, vet/necropsy records).
*Ensure proper forensic veterinary documentation is completed and retained.

2. Charge and prosecute Hicks to the fullest extent permitted by law.
*No quiet slap on the wrist.
*No informal “warning,” plea-down, or leniency that teaches abusers they can do it again.

3. Ensure the neglected dogs are treated as victims.
*Seize any animals still at risk.
*Provide veterinary assessment and documented care.
*Pursue any applicable neglect and cruelty counts alongside the shooting, not in place of it.
*Seek court-ordered restrictions on future ownership/possession as part of sentencing.

4. Use this case as an explicit public example: Hopkins County has zero tolerance for animal abuse.
*Issue a public statement confirming the case is being handled as cruelty, that evidence is being preserved, and that charges will be pursued where supported by the facts.
*Make clear this is the standard going forward.

Animal cruelty is serious violence, and treating it like “low priority” is how you manufacture the next headline. The FBI started collecting animal cruelty as a tracked offense in NIBRS because it’s not “just an animal issue,” it’s a public safety signal.

The stats are ugly for a reason. In domestic violence contexts, up to 71% of women entering shelters report their abuser threatened, injured, or killed family pets. In studies of animal cruelty cases, 70% of defendants had other criminal charges/records. And 78% of surveyed law enforcement officers agreed animal abusers are more likely to commit other crimes. When authorities respond weakly, it doesn’t “de-escalate” anything, it broadcasts permission. 

A dog was tied to a tree and shot point-blank three times, with minutes between shots. That is deliberate, sustained violence. If Hopkins County wants to claim zero tolerance, this is the moment to prove it, publicly, decisively, and with consequences that make the next would-be abuser think twice.

Hopkins County now has a choice:

Either enforce the law and deter the next abuse, or communicate that cruelty is tolerated until it escalates.

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.descriptionLong:
On December 22nd, 2025, Baghdad’s municipality admitted it carried out 1,562 “campaigns” and killed 19,386 stray dogs between Jan 1 and Nov 30, 2025.

Hamzah Barani Shamsheer was bitten in early November 2025 in Baghdad’s Ameriyah area and later died of rabies because he didn't complete the vaccination course. A child is left fatherless because his dad was an idiot.

Iraq’s Health Ministry says rabies vaccines and antiserum are available across health institutions.
So the response to one man's ignorance is collective punishment of an entire species.

Culling doesn’t “fix” street dogs. It just creates a revolving door: new dogs move in, breeding continues, and the killing becomes permanent policy.

Street dogs are a man-made problem. Abandonment creates them. Negligence feeds them. Garbage and lack of enforcement keep them there. Then authorities pretend the dogs are the problem when they become an inconvenience.

Stop the cull. Fund mass dog vaccination and CNVR/TNVR. Make bite treatment and follow-up unavoidable. Fix waste and abandonment.
links.mailtoSubject: Stop the mass killing of stray dogs in Iraq
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

Reports indicate that large-scale killing of stray dogs is being carried out in Iraq in the name of public safety following a rabies incident.

This response is both inhumane and ineffective. Indiscriminate culling does not eliminate rabies risk. The evidence-based approach is straightforward: ensure rapid access to human post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP), vaccinate dogs at scale, and manage dog populations humanely.

We demand the following actions:

1. Immediate moratorium on poisoning/shooting/indiscriminate killing of street dogs (including any municipal "campaigns").
2. A funded One Health rabies plan led jointly by Public Health and Veterinary Services.
3. Mass dog vaccination with a clear target (at least 70% coverage), paired with CNVR (catch-neuter-vaccinate-return) and humane shelter/rescue partnerships.
4. Guaranteed, publicly communicated access to human rabies PEP (vaccines + immunoglobulin where indicated), with clear guidance for bite victims.
5. Waste management and enforcement measures that address the root drivers of street-dog populations.
6. Transparency: publish monthly data on bites, PEP stock/access, vaccination numbers, CNVR outcomes, and complaints/inspections.
7. Independent oversight and an explicit ban on inhumane methods (especially poisoning).

Public health is not a firing squad. Rabies prevention is a medical and veterinary job, not a mass cruelty exercise.

Sincerely,



@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Lancaster, CA Strays
links.descriptionLong:
From Skid Row, to Stockton, to communities like Lancaster, California is starting to look like it is competing to become the street dog capital of the United States.
These dogs didn't "end up" on the streets; someone put them there. "Stray problems" are a man-made issue.
links.mailtoSubject: Lancaster Dumped Dog Pack
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing to demand immediate, sustained enforcement action regarding a growing stray and dumped-dog crisis in Lancaster / Antelope Valley, specifically around 190th Street and Avenue G where residents are reporting a pack of clearly dumped pets, some injured, some with open wounds, and at least one dog found in a condition so severe it should shame anyone in a public safety role into moving faster.

This is abandonment, neglect, and cruelty. Injured animals don't "resolve themselves." They suffer, they deteriorate, they die.

You can point to programs, policies, and "reporting channels.", but what people see on the ground is slow responses, no visible follow-through, and animals left to rot. That's the predictable result of a system that has decided cruelty can be "managed" instead of stopped.

Let's be very clear: when dogs are being dumped in Lancaster and turning into roaming packs, when animals with visible injuries are left without medical intervention, it's a failure of enforcement and basic competence.

From Skid Row, to Stockton, to communities like Lancaster, California is starting to look like it is competing to become the street dog capital of the United States. Dogs don't become "street dogs" by magic. They become street dogs through human neglect, abandonment, backyard breeding, refusal to sterilize, refusal to provide medical care, and the quiet acceptance that suffering is "normal" as long as it is not happening on someone's front lawn.

These are ongoing violations of California animal cruelty laws, including Penal Code 597, and they require real enforcement, not case numbers that go nowhere and not "we will keep an eye on it" while dogs bleed out in the dirt.

Here is what needs to happen:

1. Immediate field response and containment at and around 190th St & Ave G: locate the pack, assess condition, and take animals into protective custody where necessary.

2. Seizure/impound must mean protection, not a death sentence. These dogs need time, medical treatment, rehabilitation, and consistent care, not a rushed conveyor belt where "impounded" quietly becomes "euthanized" because it is easier. If your answer to dumped, injured pets is to scoop them up and dispose of them, you aren't solving cruelty, you're just moving it out of sight.

3. Veterinary intervention with documentation for any injured animals recovered: wounds photographed, condition recorded, and cases flagged for cruelty/neglect investigation. If you can't build cases, dumpers and abusers learn there is no consequence.

4. A coordinated cruelty investigation: canvass the area for dumping patterns, speak with residents, identify repeat dumping sites, and pull available camera footage where possible. These dogs did not appear by coincidence.

5. Target the source: dumping does not happen in a vacuum. If backyard breeding is feeding abandonment, treat it as an enforcement priority. If repeat offenders are known, treat them like repeat offenders, with citations, bans, and referrals for prosecution.

6. Public statements and transparent outcomes. Issue a clear public statement that this pack/dumping situation is being treated as cruelty and public safety, and publish a brief summary of outcomes: animals recovered, condition assessed, veterinary care provided, citations issued, and cases referred for prosecution. The public should not have to rely on rumor and graphic photos to know whether the system did its job.

Animal cruelty is never excusable. It's not made acceptable by distance from downtown, and it's not made acceptable by bureaucracy. If residents can identify a hotspot and produce proof of injured animals, then the agencies paid to protect public safety and animal welfare can respond with more than delay and silence.

Stop managing the optics. Start protecting animals.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1RGI2SoL5oWrKFWKC1OhUjdE9ACRutciB/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

HallieForAnimals

links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing regarding the publicly reported animal cruelty case involving George Christopher Carrasco of Kerrville / Kerr County.

Public posts and local bulletins describe multiple dogs found in severe neglect conditions (including emaciation). If those facts are accurate, this is not a “pet owner mistake” — it is criminal cruelty, and it should be treated like it.

This email is a demand for public accountability and full enforcement.

WHAT WE ARE DEMANDING (PUBLICLY):

1. Full prosecution to the maximum extent permitted by Texas law, with no quiet reduction that minimizes the harm.
2. Where multiple animals were involved, pursue the highest appropriate charges and counts supported by the evidence.
3. A court-ordered lifetime (or maximum-allowable) ban on owning/possessing animals, plus meaningful compliance checks.
4. Restitution for veterinary care, rehabilitation, and rescue/shelter costs.
5. A clear public record of outcomes: charges filed, case status, plea terms (if any), sentencing, and any animal-ownership restrictions.
6. A PUBLIC statement (press release and/or official social media post) confirming:
   a) the charge(s) filed,
   b) the cause/case number(s) the public can track,
   c) whether an animal possession ban and restitution are being sought,
   d) how many animals were involved (once confirmed),
   e) where the animals are now (in general terms, safe placement / rescue / shelter) and what safeguards are in place.

Public confidence requires public confirmation.

Kerr County has the chance to send a simple message: if you starve, neglect, or abandon animals here, the consequence is real, not a shrug and a fine.

Sincerely,

links.title: Protect Bakersfield's Dogs
links.descriptionLong:
Dogs are dumped with no repercussions in Bakersfield. Those dogs breed, leading to more dogs roaming the streets.

@bakersfieldstrays are on the front lines every day, doing all they can for these dogs. But until the city and authorities start doing their jobs properly, the dogs will continue to pay the price for human negligence.
links.mailtoSubject: Bakersfield: enforce dog dumping laws
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

Bakersfield is not a dumping ground.

Dogs are being abandoned and left to survive in fields, industrial edges, and vacant lots. That turns into breeding, packs, injury, disease, and preventable euthanasia. The public sees the same pattern over and over: people dump dogs, nothing happens, and the consequences land on the animals and the shelter system.

This is a law-enforcement and prevention issue. Treat it like one.

Animal abandonment is illegal in California (Penal Code 597s). If the penalty is not enforced, it gives would be abandoners the go ahead to dump their dogs, knowing they won't face any consequences.

Here is what we are demanding from the City of Bakersfield:

1. Enforce abandonment and neglect laws with real consequences

- Prioritize investigations for dumping cases when evidence exists (video, plates, addresses, witness statements).
- Issue citations and pursue prosecution where appropriate.
- Publicly report results monthly: number of dumping/abandonment complaints, cases opened, citations issued, arrests, prosecutions, and outcomes.

2. Build a prevention wall: FREE spay/neuter drives at scale

- Expand truly FREE spay/neuter events in the highest-intake areas and add weekend/evening options.
- Use mobile clinics and pop-up sites (parks, community centers, school lots).
- Promote the drives aggressively in English and Spanish, and make sign-ups simple.
- Partner with KCAS, local vets, and reputable rescues to increase surgery volume.

3. Stop the repeat-offender cycle

- Require spay/neuter before reclaim for intact animals unless there is a legitimate licensed exemption.
- Require microchip + licensing on reclaim.
- Escalate penalties for repeat owners who keep generating the same problem.

4. Make it measurable and time-bound
   Within 30 days: publish a public plan (dates, locations, and capacity) for free spay/neuter events for the next 90 days.
   Within 60 days: publish the first enforcement transparency report.
   Within 90 days: show increased sterilization numbers and enforcement actions tied to abandonment.

Dogs should not be left to rot in fields because adults refuse basic responsibility. Bakersfield has the authority to change the incentives: enforce the law so dumping stops being “cheap,” and fund prevention so unwanted litters don’t keep flooding the system.

Sincerely,

- If breeding is occurring, it may violate City of Los Angeles breeding permit requirements (LAMC 53.15.2) and could involve additional compliance issues (licensing, intact animals, veterinary care, sanitation, and animal limits).

3) Assess body condition and overall health of all animals on the property.

* No reliable access to clean drinking water
* No reliable access to adequate food
* No adequate shelter from weather
* No bed/insulated resting surface
* Forced to live outside on a concrete floor

If true, this is not "suboptimal pet ownership." It is neglect, and it is ongoing.

We demand:

1. Immediate welfare check at the address above.
2. Immediate removal and veterinary assessment if the dog is found to be dehydrated, underweight, ill, injured, lactating without proper care, or otherwise suffering.
3. Investigation and enforcement to the fullest extent of California law, including citation/seizure and referral for prosecution where evidence supports it.
4. Clear agency responsibility: do not bounce this between departments while the animal continues to be kept without basics.

Because animal cruelty reports are routinely delayed or quietly closed out with no public visibility, we are demanding a PUBLIC statement from the responsible agency/agencies confirming, at minimum:

1. That this report was received and assigned a reference/case number.
2. That a welfare check was conducted (or is scheduled), including the date/time window.
3. Whether the animal was found to have adequate water, food, and shelter at the time of the visit.
4. Whether enforcement action was taken (warning, citation, seizure, referral), as warranted by facts observed.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1FszFcrlQL7mhBPRrB6aw3RutkrnqWC9b/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

Dogs metabolize alcohol differently to us, we may get a buzz, a dog could slip into a coma or die.
links.mailtoSubject: Procesar a Aramberri por maltrato animal con whisky/Prosecute Aramberri dog whisky abuse
links.mailtoBody:
A quien corresponda:

Este correo electrónico se refiere al vídeo de Aramberri, Nuevo León, en el que unos hombres sujetan a un perro y le obligan a beber whisky mientras se ríen y graban.

Los medios de comunicación mexicanos informan de que los hombres son empleados del Departamento Forestal de la UAAAN y que esto ocurrió en un negocio de Aramberri que el gobernador prometió cerrar, afirmando que los responsables se enfrentarían a «todo el peso de la ley».

Para que esto sea creíble, se requieren las siguientes medidas:

1. Investigación penal y cargos
Tratar esto como crueldad animal deliberada, no como una broma.
Confirmar una causa penal en virtud de la versión aplicable del artículo 445 del Código Penal de Nuevo León.
Indicar si se ha identificado, interrogado y acusado a los hombres, y aplicar las penas más severas disponibles, incluidas multas, custodia cuando sea posible y la prohibición de poseer o manejar animales.

2. Sanciones administrativas.
Confirmar que el negocio ha sido cerrado y sancionado según lo prometido, y publicar la base legal y la duración.

3. Medidas de la UAAAN.
Iniciar procedimientos disciplinarios, suspender a las personas de sus funciones relacionadas con animales o que representen a la universidad, y emitir una declaración pública de que esta conducta es incompatible con su misión.

Sin una acción clara, las reformas que reconocen a los animales como seres sensibles parecen más una cuestión de relaciones públicas que de protección. Obligar a un perro a ingerir alcohol mientras está inmovilizado es crueldad, no humor.

Por favor, hagan una declaración pública sobre el procedimiento en nombre de la transparencia.

Evidencia: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1OsItT-xqq6tv28J-KKie6nJc3Rk0c4jd/view?usp=sharing

Atentamente,

To whom it may concern,

This email concerns the video from Aramberri, Nuevo Leon, where men hold a dog down and force whisky into its mouth while laughing and filming.

Mexican media report that the men are employees of the Forestry Department of UAAAN and that this happened in a business in Aramberri that the Governor promised to close, saying those responsible would face "all the weight of the law".

For that to be credible, the following actions are required:

1. Criminal investigation and charges
Treat this as deliberate animal cruelty, not a joke.
Confirm a criminal case under the applicable version of Article 445 of the Penal Code of Nuevo Leon.
State whether the men have been identified, interviewed, and charged, and apply the strongest penalties available, including fines, custody where possible, and a ban on owning or handling animals.

2. Administrative sanctions
Confirm that the business has been closed and sanctioned as promised, and publish the legal basis and duration.

3. Measures from UAAAN
Open disciplinary proceedings, suspend the individuals from roles involving animals or representing the university, and issue a public statement that this conduct is incompatible with its mission.

Without clear action, reforms recognising animals as sentient beings look like public relations, not protection. Forcing a dog to ingest alcohol while restrained is cruelty, not humour.

Please make a public statement on the proceedings in the name of transparency.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1OsItT-xqq6tv28J-KKie6nJc3Rk0c4jd/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

links.title: Neglected Dog on Tupelo Street Louisiana

links.mailtoSubject: Dog tethered outside all day at 2613 Tupelo St
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing to report a dog who appears to be neglected and illegally tethered at:

2613 Tupelo Street
Lake Charles, LA 70601

On multiple recent days, a dog has been left tied up outside all day at this address while the owner is away at work at a nursing home kitchen near Ernest St and 18th. The dog is left alone for the duration of the workday, tethered in the yard with no visible supervision.

From what has been observed:

* The dog is restrained outside for many hours at a time.
* There appears to be no reliable access to proper shelter from sun, heat, rain, or cold.
* It is not clear that the dog has continuous access to clean drinking water.
* The dog is alone and tethered for the full work shift, not just brief periods.

Under Louisiana law, it is illegal to keep animals in conditions that cause unnecessary suffering or deny them basic needs. Louisiana cruelty statutes require that animals be provided with proper food, water, and shelter and prohibit mistreatment that leads to unjustifiable pain, suffering, or risk of harm.

In addition, Louisiana tethering provisions make it unlawful to tie or restrain an animal in a way that is inhumane, cruel, or detrimental to its welfare, and further restrict leaving dogs tethered outside without adequate shelter, shade, drinkable water, and protection from the weather.

Local officials in Calcasieu Parish have also publicly stated that animals may not be left outside unattended unless kept in an approved tether system or secure confinement that prevents escape and protects them. A dog tied up and left alone all day with minimal or no shelter and water is clearly not in line with those standards.

I am therefore requesting:

1. An immediate welfare check at 2613 Tupelo Street, Lake Charles, LA 70601.
2. Confirmation that the dog has:

   * Adequate, lawful shelter (solid walls and roof, dry floor, space to stand, turn, and lie down),
   * Continuous access to clean water,
   * Protection from heat, cold, and rain, and
   * A tethering setup, if used, that is not inhumane or detrimental to the dog’s welfare.
3. If your investigation confirms violations of Louisiana law or parish ordinances, I am asking that:

   * The dog be removed to safety if necessary, and
   * Appropriate charges be pursued against the person responsible under the relevant cruelty and tethering statutes.

Please treat this as a priority and provide a public statement outlining:

* Whether a welfare check has been carried out,
* What was found at the property, and
* What action, if any, will be taken to protect this dog and enforce the law.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1OvYa5fjpjH1Hqn5BunlJ-OCnPoqHCJcy/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

links.title: Justice For Fluffo
links.descriptionLong:
Fluffo was a good boy, a happy boy, a bouncy boy. Yet someone set him on fire and left his body on full display.

The Stockton street dog situation looks like something out of a third world country, not the economic powerhouse of the United States.
Hundreds of dogs are chained, starving, and dehydrated. No food, no water, no shelter, no room to move. Abuse is happening openly while the people in charge stand by and watch.
In just a couple of weekends, two separate brand-new litters of puppies appeared and had to be rushed into homes so they didn’t end up dead or back in the same nightmare.
And through all of it, there is only one consistent presence on the ground: @freedomdogco run by @cydnikeala who are the only ones trying to hold back a tidal wave of suffering while those with the power and the budgets look away and line their pockets with taxpayer money.
links.mailtoSubject: Fluffo the street dog burned to death in Stockton
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

On the morning of December 9th, within the industrial corridor in south Stockton, residents found the burned body of a street dog known as Fluffo. He was discovered under a scorched board, his body charred, his paws and fur still visible. This was deliberate torture, carried out in public and left on display.

If Stockton cannot treat this as a serious violent crime, the system is sending a very clear message that you can set an animal on fire in this city and nobody in power will take it seriously.

This isn't just about a dog.

Law enforcement and court systems across the U.S. already recognise that animal cruelty is tightly linked to human violence.

The FBI now classifies animal cruelty as a Group A offense and a “crime against society,” in the same reporting category as arson and homicide, and tracks it nationally for that reason.

Researchers for the National Council of Juvenile and Family Court Judges report that around 43% of U.S. school shooters between 1988 and 2012 had histories of animal abuse.

Congressional testimony and domestic-violence research have found that up to 71% of women entering domestic-violence shelters say their abuser has threatened, injured, or killed the family pet.

National “Link” organisations and child-protection bodies describe animal abuse as part of a continuum of family violence that includes domestic abuse, child abuse, and elder abuse.

The person who set Fluffo on fire isn't “someone who doesn't like dogs.” They're exactly the kind of offender every cop, prosecutor, and judge is supposed to be watching for.

Fluffo wasn't “just a stray.” He was a victim in a city where animals are left to suffer on sidewalks while officials go home to safe houses, safe families, and well-cared-for pets. If Stockton PD and city leadership cannot protect a friendly, unarmed dog from being burned alive, it is hard to believe you are protecting the children and partners living on the same streets.

We are demanding, at minimum, the following:

1. Immediate classification and handling of this case as felony animal cruelty, with a named lead investigator.

2. A full evidence workup, including:

   * collection and review of available CCTV, Ring, and security footage;
   * a canvas of nearby homes and businesses;
   * a public appeal for witnesses.

3. A public statement confirming:

   * that this case is being actively investigated;
   * the case number and point of contact;
   * what steps are being taken to identify the offender;
   * how the public can submit tips, photos, or video.

4. An explanation of policy: how Stockton PD and partner agencies track animal-cruelty cases and cross-reference them with domestic violence, child abuse, and other violent crime, in line with what federal agencies and other jurisdictions are already doing.

Right now, the message on the ground is simple: stray dogs can be starved, beaten, chained, and now burned alive, and no one in authority feels any urgency to act. That is not “public safety.” It is a warning sign you are ignoring.

Fluffo is gone. You cannot save him. What you can do is prove that what happened to him matters; that Stockton understands what this kind of cruelty means; and that you are prepared to act before the person who burned a dog to death in your city escalates to their next victim.

We expect a prompt, public response detailing what is being done in this case and how you intend to prevent the next one.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1N9pdgzaQqTGVwYgnslldqYmyVTIxtQit/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,

links.title: Odelia Asher Dog Abuse
links.descriptionLong:

Odelia is a realtor at Rodeo Realty and owns Odelia's Wellness Beverly Hills

links.mailtoSubject: Demand for Welfare Check & Cruelty Review
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing to request an immediate welfare check and renewed cruelty review regarding a dog in the custody of Odelia Asher (also known as Odelia Bustnay), a Beverly Hills–area realtor and wellness practitioner.

Public information lists Ms Asher as a real estate agent with Rodeo Realty in Beverly Hills and the owner of "Odelia's Wellness Beverly Hills." She is believed to reside at or regularly occupy the following Los Angeles addresses:

* 1547 N Hayworth Ave, Unit 101, Los Angeles, CA 90046
* 10635 Wilshire Blvd, Unit 301, Los Angeles, CA 90024

Recently, a video circulated online showing Ms Asher handling her dog with extreme aggression in public. In that video, she appears to violently yank the dog by the neck/leash area as the animal resists and displays clear signs of fear and distress. Members of the public who reported this to animal control were allegedly told that the footage "does not meet the legal criteria for abuse."

If this level of physical aggression is what she feels comfortable doing on the street, the obvious question is: what is happening to this dog behind closed doors?

Under California Penal Code 597, cruelty is not limited to killing or maiming. It also covers:

* Cruelly beating an animal, or
* Subjecting an animal in your care to needless suffering or unnecessary cruelty, or
* Otherwise abusing an animal in your custody.

The statute does not require the dog to be at death's door for the conduct to be criminal. A pattern of violent handling that causes pain and fear can still violate the law.

Los Angeles agencies are well aware of the link between animal cruelty and violence against humans. In households with prosecutable animal cruelty, children and partners are at significantly higher risk of abuse. Animal cruelty is a public safety issue, not a trivial matter. Dismissing clear evidence of violent handling with "it does not meet criteria" is not an acceptable endpoint here.

I am therefore requesting the following:

1. Immediate in-person welfare check

   * Conduct a prompt, unannounced welfare check on all animals in Ms Asher's custody at:

     * 1547 N Hayworth Ave, Unit 101, Los Angeles, CA 90046
     * 10635 Wilshire Blvd, Unit 301, Los Angeles, CA 90024
   * Verify:

     * Body condition, injuries, or pain responses
     * Condition and fit of any collar or harness
     * Living environment (space, cleanliness, confinement, access to food, water, and exercise)
   * Photograph and document all findings.

2. Formal review of the video evidence by the Animal Cruelty Task Force and DA's Office

   * I ask that the LAPD/LA Animal Cruelty Task Force (ACTF) and Los Angeles County District Attorney's Office review the video in light of Penal Code 597, especially the language about "cruelly beating" and "subjecting an animal to needless suffering or unnecessary cruelty."
   * If animal control previously declined to act, that determination should be revisited and documented.

3. Clarification of "legal criteria" being applied

   * Please explain what threshold is being used when an owner is caught on camera violently yanking a dog and the animal is clearly fearful, but the case is dismissed as "not meeting criteria."

4. Case creation and tracking

   * Please open a formal case file for this incident so that:

     * Future reports involving this individual or address are linked, and
     * There is a record that a welfare concern was raised, not quietly discarded.
   * If you determine that the conduct does not rise to criminal prosecution at this time, Ms Asher should at minimum receive a documented warning and education on humane handling standards.

This is not a "training style" dispute. It is a question of whether a dog in Ms Asher's custody is being subjected to violent, fear-based handling that would clearly be illegal if done to a child or a frail adult. California law recognises animals as sentient beings capable of suffering; your enforcement decisions should reflect that.

This email should be treated as both:

* A request for an immediate welfare check, and
* A complaint regarding the apparent failure to treat obvious abusive handling as a potential Penal Code 597 violation.

I expect a public statement from the responsible agencies after the welfare check and legal review have been completed. At a minimum, that public statement should clearly set out:

* Whether the dog was examined and what the welfare findings were;
* Whether the video and associated conduct are being treated as a potential violation of Penal Code 597, and if not, precisely why;
* What internal procedures, training, and enforcement criteria you are using to distinguish "no action" cases from cases that trigger investigation and prosecution; and
* What safeguards you are putting in place to ensure that if there is further abuse of this dog, or other animals in Ms Asher's custody, those failures cannot be blamed on a prior decision to look the other way.

The public has the right to know why a dog can be violently yanked in public, on video, and be told it "does not meet criteria" for abuse. You are accountable for those criteria, and for the consequences of getting this wrong.

Evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1fOnSkjWJdIYCs8-A2NFRfirMmPzsMvjd/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,
